name: wonda

metadata.name: wonda
metadata.summary: A light, blazing fast framework for developing capable bots.

documentation:
# Wonda

[//]: # "Links"
[examples]: examples/
[format text]: examples/text_styling_example.py
[upload files]: examples/file_upload_example.py
[an advanced state system]: examples/use_state_manager.py
[translate your bot to dozens of languages]: examples/internationalization_example.py

[//]: # "Badges"
![Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/wonda?label=version&style=flat-square)
![Package downloads](https://img.shields.io/pypi/dw/wonda?label=downloads&style=flat-square)
![Supported Python versions](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/wonda?label=supported%20python%20versions&style=flat-square)

Wonda is a Telegram bot framework. It's light, blazing fast and feature rich. It also supports latest Bot API versions immediately. [Join our Telegram forum](https://t.me/wndrgrm)

## Install

```bash
pip install wonda
```

A stable version of Wonda can be installed using this command. Add `--pre` to the command to install a pre-release version.

## Features

You can build a basic bot in only 6 lines of code:

```python
from wonda import Bot, Message

bot = Bot("your-token")


@bot.on.message()
async def handler(m: Message) -> None:
    await m.answer("Hello world!")

bot.run_forever()
```

You can also [format text], [upload files], [translate your bot to dozens of languages] and make use of [an advanced state system] to build complex interactions. To learn more, check out the [examples].

## Customization

Wonda ships with few dependencies by default. To customize your experience, you may want to install some extra dependencies on top.

### Speed up event loop

```bash
pip install uvloop
```

Wonda supports uvloop, an event loop implementation which makes I/O [2-4x quicker](https://github.com/magicstack/uvloop#performance).

### Replace default JSON module

```bash
pip install orjson
```

Swap out the module that is used under the hood to manipulate JSON and increase speed up to [5 times](https://github.com/ijl/orjson#performance).

### Display tracebacks in greater detail

```bash
pip install rich
```

Check out [rich](https://github.com/textualize/rich), a library which provides amazing rich text capabilities for your logs.

## Contributing

This framework is a robust solution for what it's worth, but the API may be unstable for a while. That means it's not yet ready for production use.

So, if you like our work, help us develop this amazing project! Here are some ways you can help.

### With code

Contributions are welcome! [File a ticket](https://github.com/wondergram-org/wonda/issues) if you have something to say about the state of the framework.

### With finances

There are a couple options available for monetary help. Click [Sponsor](https://boosty.to/geo_madness) to see them. Thank you!

## License

This project exists thanks to the amazing work done by timoniq. Code is licensed under [MIT](LICENSE).

Type of the chat, can be either "private", "group", "supergroup" or
"channel"

documentation:
Type of the chat, can be either "private", "group", "supergroup" or

classes.name: wonda.types.enums.MessageEntityType
documentation:
Type of the entity. Currently, can be "mention" (@username), "hashtag"
(#hashtag or #hashtag@chatusername), "cashtag" ($USD or
$USD@chatusername), "bot_command" (/start@jobs_bot), "url"
(https://telegram.org), "email" (do-not-reply@telegram.org),
"phone_number" (+1-212-555-0123), "bold" (bold text), "italic" (italic
text), "underline" (underlined text), "strikethrough" (strikethrough
text), "spoiler" (spoiler message), "blockquote" (block quotation),
"expandable_blockquote" (collapsed-by-default block quotation), "code"
(monowidth string), "pre" (monowidth block), "text_link" (for
clickable text URLs), "text_mention" (for users without usernames),
"custom_emoji" (for inline custom emoji stickers)

classes.name: wonda.types.enums.PollType
documentation: Poll type, currently can be "regular" or "quiz"

classes.name: wonda.types.enums.StickerType
documentation:
Type of the sticker, currently one of "regular", "mask",
"custom_emoji". The type of the sticker is independent from its
format, which is determined by the fields is_animated and is_video.

classes.name: wonda.types.enums.StickerSetStickerType
documentation:
Type of stickers in the set, currently one of "regular", "mask",
"custom_emoji"

classes.name: wonda.types.enums.MaskPositionPoint
documentation:
The part of the face relative to which the mask should be placed. One
of "forehead", "eyes", "mouth", or "chin".

classes.name: wonda.types.enums.InputStickerFormat
documentation:
Format of the added sticker, must be one of "static" for a .WEBP or
.PNG image, "animated" for a .TGS animation, "video" for a .WEBM video

classes.name: wonda.types.enums.InlineQueryChatType
documentation:
Optional. Type of the chat from which the inline query was sent. Can
be either "sender" for a private chat with the inline query sender,
"private", "group", "supergroup", or "channel". The chat type should
be always known for requests sent from official clients and most
third-party clients, unless the request was sent from a secret chat

classes.name: wonda.types.enums.InlineQueryResultGifThumbnailMimeType
documentation:
Optional. MIME type of the thumbnail, must be one of "image/jpeg",
"image/gif", or "video/mp4". Defaults to "image/jpeg"

classes.name: wonda.types.enums.InlineQueryResultMpeg4GifThumbnailMimeType

Optional. MIME type of the thumbnail, must be one of "image/jpeg",
"image/gif", or "video/mp4". Defaults to "image/jpeg"

classes.name: wonda.types.enums.InlineQueryResultVideoMimeType

classes.name: wonda.types.enums.InlineQueryResultDocumentMimeType

"application/zip"

class_variables.name: wonda.bot.dispatch.dispatcher.abc.ABCDispatcher.message
documentation:
This view is used to handle message updates. It receives `message` and
`edited_message` updates. Edited message updates can be sent even if
the changed fields are not available to your bot.

class_variables.name: wonda.bot.dispatch.dispatcher.abc.ABCDispatcher.channel_post
documentation:
A view for handling channel posts. It receives `channel_post` and
`edited_channel_post` updates. Edited channel post updates can be 
sent even if the changed fields are not available to your bot.

class_variables.name: wonda.bot.dispatch.dispatcher.abc.ABCDispatcher.business_connection
documentation:
This view is used to handle updates related to business connections.
For example, the connection or disconnection of the bot from
a business account.

class_variables.name: wonda.bot.dispatch.dispatcher.abc.ABCDispatcher.business_message
documentation:
A view for handling messages from connected business accounts.
It receives `business_message` and `edited_business_message` updates.
Edited business message updates can be sent even if the changed fields 
are not available to your bot.

class_variables.name: wonda.bot.dispatch.dispatcher.abc.ABCDispatcher.deleted_business_messages
documentation: This view is used to handle messages deleted from a business account.

class_variables.name: wonda.bot.dispatch.dispatcher.abc.ABCDispatcher.chat_member
documentation:
This view is for handling changes to the state of a chat member,
such as joining or leaving a chat. It receives `chat_member`
and `my_chat_member` updates.

class_variables.name: wonda.bot.dispatch.dispatcher.abc.ABCDispatcher.callback_query
documentation:
A view for handling callback queries from inline buttons. If the button
that originated the query is attached to a message sent in inline mode,
the field `inline_message_id` will be present.

class_variables.name: wonda.bot.dispatch.dispatcher.abc.ABCDispatcher.inline_query
documentation:
This view is used to handle an incoming inline query. An inline query is sent
when the user starts typing the bot's username in the message field. Inline
mode must be enabled in @Botfather.

class_variables.name: wonda.bot.dispatch.dispatcher.abc.ABCDispatcher.message_reaction
documentation:
A view for handling message reactions. The bot must be an administrator
in the chat to receive such updates. Reactions set by bots can't be
handled.

class_variables.name: wonda.bot.dispatch.dispatcher.abc.ABCDispatcher.message_reaction_count
documentation:
This view is used to handle reactions set to anonymous messages. The bot
must be an administrator in the chat to receive these updates. Reaction
count updates are grouped and sent with a delay of several minutes.

class_variables.name: wonda.bot.dispatch.dispatcher.abc.ABCDispatcher.chat_join_request
documentation:
A view for handling requests to join the chat. The bot must have
`can_invite_users` right in the chat to receive these updates.

class_variables.name: wonda.bot.dispatch.dispatcher.abc.ABCDispatcher.chosen_inline_result
documentation:
This view is used to handle a result of an inline query that was
chosen by the user and sent to their chat partner. Feedback
collection must be enabled in @Botfather.

class_variables.name: wonda.bot.dispatch.dispatcher.abc.ABCDispatcher.shipping_query
documentation:
A view for handling shipping queries. This update is sent only
if the invoice has a flexible price.

class_variables.name: wonda.bot.dispatch.dispatcher.abc.ABCDispatcher.pre_checkout_query
documentation: This view is used to handle pre-checkout queries.

class_variables.name: wonda.bot.dispatch.dispatcher.abc.ABCDispatcher.purchased_paid_media
documentation: A view for handling paid media purchased by a user in a non-channel chat.

class_variables.name: wonda.bot.dispatch.dispatcher.abc.ABCDispatcher.chat_boost
documentation:
A view for handling changes to chat boosts. The bot must be an administrator
in the chat to receive these updates.

class_variables.name: wonda.bot.dispatch.dispatcher.abc.ABCDispatcher.removed_chat_boost
documentation:
This view is used to handle boosts that were removed from a chat.
The bot must be an administrator in the chat to receive these updates.

class_variables.name: wonda.bot.dispatch.dispatcher.abc.ABCDispatcher.poll_answer
documentation:
A view for handling user votes in non-anonymous polls. The bot will receive
votes only in the polls that were sent by the bot itself.

class_variables.name: wonda.bot.dispatch.dispatcher.abc.ABCDispatcher.poll
documentation:
This view handles updates to polls, including content modifications 
and manual closures.
An interface for distributing received updates to their respective views.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.get_updates
documentation:
Use this method to receive incoming updates using long polling (wiki).
Returns an Array of Update objects.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.set_webhook
documentation:
Use this method to specify a URL and receive incoming updates via an
outgoing webhook. Whenever there is an update for the bot, we will
send an HTTPS POST request to the specified URL, containing a JSON-
serialized Update. In case of an unsuccessful request (a request with
response HTTP status code different from 2XY), we will repeat the
request and give up after a reasonable amount of attempts. Returns
True on success. If you'd like to make sure that the webhook was set
by you, you can specify secret data in the parameter secret_token. If
specified, the request will contain a header "X-Telegram-Bot-Api-
Secret-Token" with the secret token as content.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.delete_webhook
documentation:
Use this method to remove webhook integration if you decide to switch
back to getUpdates. Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.get_webhook_info
documentation:
Use this method to get current webhook status. Requires no parameters.
On success, returns a WebhookInfo object. If the bot is using
getUpdates, will return an object with the url field empty.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.get_me
documentation:
A simple method for testing your bot's authentication token. Requires
no parameters. Returns basic information about the bot in form of a
User object.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.log_out
documentation:
Use this method to log out from the cloud Bot API server before
launching the bot locally. You must log out the bot before running it
locally, otherwise there is no guarantee that the bot will receive
updates. After a successful call, you can immediately log in on a
local server, but will not be able to log in back to the cloud Bot API
server for 10 minutes. Returns True on success. Requires no
parameters.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.close
documentation:
Use this method to close the bot instance before moving it from one
local server to another. You need to delete the webhook before calling
this method to ensure that the bot isn't launched again after server
restart. The method will return error 429 in the first 10 minutes
after the bot is launched. Returns True on success. Requires no
parameters.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.send_message
documentation:
Use this method to send text messages. On success, the sent Message is
returned.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.forward_message
documentation:
Use this method to forward messages of any kind. Service messages and
messages with protected content can't be forwarded. On success, the
sent Message is returned.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.forward_messages
documentation:
Use this method to forward multiple messages of any kind. If some of
the specified messages can't be found or forwarded, they are skipped.
Service messages and messages with protected content can't be
forwarded. Album grouping is kept for forwarded messages. On success,
an array of MessageId of the sent messages is returned.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.copy_message
documentation:
Use this method to copy messages of any kind. Service messages, paid
media messages, giveaway messages, giveaway winners messages, and
invoice messages can't be copied. A quiz poll can be copied only if
the value of the field correct_option_id is known to the bot. The
method is analogous to the method forwardMessage, but the copied
message doesn't have a link to the original message. Returns the
MessageId of the sent message on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.copy_messages
documentation:
Use this method to copy messages of any kind. If some of the specified
messages can't be found or copied, they are skipped. Service messages,
paid media messages, giveaway messages, giveaway winners messages, and
invoice messages can't be copied. A quiz poll can be copied only if
the value of the field correct_option_id is known to the bot. The
method is analogous to the method forwardMessages, but the copied
messages don't have a link to the original message. Album grouping is
kept for copied messages. On success, an array of MessageId of the
sent messages is returned.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.send_photo
documentation:
Use this method to send photos. On success, the sent Message is
returned.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.send_audio
documentation:
Use this method to send audio files, if you want Telegram clients to
display them in the music player. Your audio must be in the .MP3 or
.M4A format. On success, the sent Message is returned. Bots can
currently send audio files of up to 50 MB in size, this limit may be
changed in the future. For sending voice messages, use the sendVoice
method instead.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.send_document
documentation:
Use this method to send general files. On success, the sent Message is
returned. Bots can currently send files of any type of up to 50 MB in
size, this limit may be changed in the future.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.send_video
documentation:
Use this method to send video files, Telegram clients support MPEG4
videos (other formats may be sent as Document). On success, the sent
Message is returned. Bots can currently send video files of up to 50
MB in size, this limit may be changed in the future.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.send_animation
documentation:
Use this method to send animation files (GIF or H.264/MPEG-4 AVC video
without sound). On success, the sent Message is returned. Bots can
currently send animation files of up to 50 MB in size, this limit may
be changed in the future.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.send_voice
documentation:
Use this method to send audio files, if you want Telegram clients to
display the file as a playable voice message. For this to work, your
audio must be in an .OGG file encoded with OPUS, or in .MP3 format, or
in .M4A format (other formats may be sent as Audio or Document). On
success, the sent Message is returned. Bots can currently send voice
messages of up to 50 MB in size, this limit may be changed in the
future.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.send_video_note
documentation:
As of v.4.0, Telegram clients support rounded square MPEG4 videos of
up to 1 minute long. Use this method to send video messages. On
success, the sent Message is returned.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.send_paid_media
documentation:
Use this method to send paid media. On success, the sent Message is
returned.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.send_media_group
documentation:
Use this method to send a group of photos, videos, documents or audios
as an album. Documents and audio files can be only grouped in an album
with messages of the same type. On success, an array of Messages that
were sent is returned.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.send_location
documentation:
Use this method to send point on the map. On success, the sent Message
is returned.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.send_venue
documentation:
Use this method to send information about a venue. On success, the
sent Message is returned.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.send_contact
documentation:
Use this method to send phone contacts. On success, the sent Message
is returned.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.send_poll
documentation:
Use this method to send a native poll. On success, the sent Message is
returned.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.send_dice
documentation:
Use this method to send an animated emoji that will display a random
value. On success, the sent Message is returned.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.send_chat_action
documentation:
Use this method when you need to tell the user that something is
happening on the bot's side. The status is set for 5 seconds or less
(when a message arrives from your bot, Telegram clients clear its
typing status). Returns True on success. Example: The ImageBot needs
some time to process a request and upload the image. Instead of
sending a text message along the lines of "Retrieving image, please
wait...", the bot may use sendChatAction with action = upload_photo.
The user will see a "sending photo" status for the bot. We only
recommend using this method when a response from the bot will take a
noticeable amount of time to arrive.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.set_message_reaction
documentation:
Use this method to change the chosen reactions on a message. Service
messages can't be reacted to. Automatically forwarded messages from a
channel to its discussion group have the same available reactions as
messages in the channel. Bots can't use paid reactions. Returns True
on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.get_user_profile_photos
documentation:
Use this method to get a list of profile pictures for a user. Returns
a UserProfilePhotos object.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.set_user_emoji_status
documentation:
Changes the emoji status for a given user that previously allowed the
bot to manage their emoji status via the Mini App method
requestEmojiStatusAccess. Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.get_file
documentation:
Use this method to get basic information about a file and prepare it
for downloading. For the moment, bots can download files of up to 20MB
in size. On success, a File object is returned. The file can then be
downloaded via the link
https://api.telegram.org/file/bot<token>/<file_path>, where
<file_path> is taken from the response. It is guaranteed that the link
will be valid for at least 1 hour. When the link expires, a new one
can be requested by calling getFile again.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.ban_chat_member
documentation:
Use this method to ban a user in a group, a supergroup or a channel.
In the case of supergroups and channels, the user will not be able to
return to the chat on their own using invite links, etc., unless
unbanned first. The bot must be an administrator in the chat for this
to work and must have the appropriate administrator rights. Returns
True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.unban_chat_member
documentation:
Use this method to unban a previously banned user in a supergroup or
channel. The user will not return to the group or channel
automatically, but will be able to join via link, etc. The bot must be
an administrator for this to work. By default, this method guarantees
that after the call the user is not a member of the chat, but will be
able to join it. So if the user is a member of the chat they will also
be removed from the chat. If you don't want this, use the parameter
only_if_banned. Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.restrict_chat_member
documentation:
Use this method to restrict a user in a supergroup. The bot must be an
administrator in the supergroup for this to work and must have the
appropriate administrator rights. Pass True for all permissions to
lift restrictions from a user. Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.promote_chat_member
documentation:
Use this method to promote or demote a user in a supergroup or a
channel. The bot must be an administrator in the chat for this to work
and must have the appropriate administrator rights. Pass False for all
boolean parameters to demote a user. Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.set_chat_administrator_custom_title
documentation:
Use this method to set a custom title for an administrator in a
supergroup promoted by the bot. Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.ban_chat_sender_chat
documentation:
Use this method to ban a channel chat in a supergroup or a channel.
Until the chat is unbanned, the owner of the banned chat won't be able
to send messages on behalf of any of their channels. The bot must be
an administrator in the supergroup or channel for this to work and
must have the appropriate administrator rights. Returns True on
success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.unban_chat_sender_chat
documentation:
Use this method to unban a previously banned channel chat in a
supergroup or channel. The bot must be an administrator for this to
work and must have the appropriate administrator rights. Returns True
on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.set_chat_permissions
documentation:
Use this method to set default chat permissions for all members. The
bot must be an administrator in the group or a supergroup for this to
work and must have the can_restrict_members administrator rights.
Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.export_chat_invite_link
documentation:
Use this method to generate a new primary invite link for a chat; any
previously generated primary link is revoked. The bot must be an
administrator in the chat for this to work and must have the
appropriate administrator rights. Returns the new invite link as
String on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.create_chat_invite_link
documentation:
Use this method to create an additional invite link for a chat. The
bot must be an administrator in the chat for this to work and must
have the appropriate administrator rights. The link can be revoked
using the method revokeChatInviteLink. Returns the new invite link as
ChatInviteLink object.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.edit_chat_invite_link
documentation:
Use this method to edit a non-primary invite link created by the bot.
The bot must be an administrator in the chat for this to work and must
have the appropriate administrator rights. Returns the edited invite
link as a ChatInviteLink object.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.create_chat_subscription_invite_link
documentation:
Use this method to create a subscription invite link for a channel
chat. The bot must have the can_invite_users administrator rights. The
link can be edited using the method editChatSubscriptionInviteLink or
revoked using the method revokeChatInviteLink. Returns the new invite
link as a ChatInviteLink object.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.edit_chat_subscription_invite_link
documentation:
Use this method to edit a subscription invite link created by the bot.
The bot must have the can_invite_users administrator rights. Returns
the edited invite link as a ChatInviteLink object.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.revoke_chat_invite_link
documentation:
Use this method to revoke an invite link created by the bot. If the
primary link is revoked, a new link is automatically generated. The
bot must be an administrator in the chat for this to work and must
have the appropriate administrator rights. Returns the revoked invite
link as ChatInviteLink object.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.approve_chat_join_request
documentation:
Use this method to approve a chat join request. The bot must be an
administrator in the chat for this to work and must have the
can_invite_users administrator right. Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.decline_chat_join_request
documentation:
Use this method to decline a chat join request. The bot must be an
administrator in the chat for this to work and must have the
can_invite_users administrator right. Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.set_chat_photo
documentation:
Use this method to set a new profile photo for the chat. Photos can't
be changed for private chats. The bot must be an administrator in the
chat for this to work and must have the appropriate administrator
rights. Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.delete_chat_photo
documentation:
Use this method to delete a chat photo. Photos can't be changed for
private chats. The bot must be an administrator in the chat for this
to work and must have the appropriate administrator rights. Returns
True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.set_chat_title
documentation:
Use this method to change the title of a chat. Titles can't be changed
for private chats. The bot must be an administrator in the chat for
this to work and must have the appropriate administrator rights.
Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.set_chat_description
documentation:
Use this method to change the description of a group, a supergroup or
a channel. The bot must be an administrator in the chat for this to
work and must have the appropriate administrator rights. Returns True
on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.pin_chat_message
documentation:
Use this method to add a message to the list of pinned messages in a
chat. If the chat is not a private chat, the bot must be an
administrator in the chat for this to work and must have the
'can_pin_messages' administrator right in a supergroup or
'can_edit_messages' administrator right in a channel. Returns True on
success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.unpin_chat_message
documentation:
Use this method to remove a message from the list of pinned messages
in a chat. If the chat is not a private chat, the bot must be an
administrator in the chat for this to work and must have the
'can_pin_messages' administrator right in a supergroup or
'can_edit_messages' administrator right in a channel. Returns True on
success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.unpin_all_chat_messages
documentation:
Use this method to clear the list of pinned messages in a chat. If the
chat is not a private chat, the bot must be an administrator in the
chat for this to work and must have the 'can_pin_messages'
administrator right in a supergroup or 'can_edit_messages'
administrator right in a channel. Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.leave_chat
documentation:
Use this method for your bot to leave a group, supergroup or channel.
Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.get_chat
documentation:
Use this method to get up-to-date information about the chat. Returns
a ChatFullInfo object on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.get_chat_administrators
documentation:
Use this method to get a list of administrators in a chat, which
aren't bots. Returns an Array of ChatMember objects.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.get_chat_member_count
documentation:
Use this method to get the number of members in a chat. Returns Int on
success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.get_chat_member
documentation:
Use this method to get information about a member of a chat. The
method is only guaranteed to work for other users if the bot is an
administrator in the chat. Returns a ChatMember object on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.set_chat_sticker_set
documentation:
Use this method to set a new group sticker set for a supergroup. The
bot must be an administrator in the chat for this to work and must
have the appropriate administrator rights. Use the field
can_set_sticker_set optionally returned in getChat requests to check
if the bot can use this method. Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.delete_chat_sticker_set
documentation:
Use this method to delete a group sticker set from a supergroup. The
bot must be an administrator in the chat for this to work and must
have the appropriate administrator rights. Use the field
can_set_sticker_set optionally returned in getChat requests to check
if the bot can use this method. Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.get_forum_topic_icon_stickers
documentation:
Use this method to get custom emoji stickers, which can be used as a
forum topic icon by any user. Requires no parameters. Returns an Array
of Sticker objects.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.create_forum_topic
documentation:
Use this method to create a topic in a forum supergroup chat. The bot
must be an administrator in the chat for this to work and must have
the can_manage_topics administrator rights. Returns information about
the created topic as a ForumTopic object.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.edit_forum_topic
documentation:
Use this method to edit name and icon of a topic in a forum supergroup
chat. The bot must be an administrator in the chat for this to work
and must have the can_manage_topics administrator rights, unless it is
the creator of the topic. Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.close_forum_topic
documentation:
Use this method to close an open topic in a forum supergroup chat. The
bot must be an administrator in the chat for this to work and must
have the can_manage_topics administrator rights, unless it is the
creator of the topic. Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.reopen_forum_topic
documentation:
Use this method to reopen a closed topic in a forum supergroup chat.
The bot must be an administrator in the chat for this to work and must
have the can_manage_topics administrator rights, unless it is the
creator of the topic. Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.delete_forum_topic
documentation:
Use this method to delete a forum topic along with all its messages in
a forum supergroup chat. The bot must be an administrator in the chat
for this to work and must have the can_delete_messages administrator
rights. Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.unpin_all_forum_topic_messages
documentation:
Use this method to clear the list of pinned messages in a forum topic.
The bot must be an administrator in the chat for this to work and must
have the can_pin_messages administrator right in the supergroup.
Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.edit_general_forum_topic
documentation:
Use this method to edit the name of the 'General' topic in a forum
supergroup chat. The bot must be an administrator in the chat for this
to work and must have the can_manage_topics administrator rights.
Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.close_general_forum_topic
documentation:
Use this method to close an open 'General' topic in a forum supergroup
chat. The bot must be an administrator in the chat for this to work
and must have the can_manage_topics administrator rights. Returns True
on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.reopen_general_forum_topic
documentation:
Use this method to reopen a closed 'General' topic in a forum
supergroup chat. The bot must be an administrator in the chat for this
to work and must have the can_manage_topics administrator rights. The
topic will be automatically unhidden if it was hidden. Returns True on
success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.hide_general_forum_topic
documentation:
Use this method to hide the 'General' topic in a forum supergroup
chat. The bot must be an administrator in the chat for this to work
and must have the can_manage_topics administrator rights. The topic
will be automatically closed if it was open. Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.unhide_general_forum_topic
documentation:
Use this method to unhide the 'General' topic in a forum supergroup
chat. The bot must be an administrator in the chat for this to work
and must have the can_manage_topics administrator rights. Returns True
on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.unpin_all_general_forum_topic_messages
documentation:
Use this method to clear the list of pinned messages in a General
forum topic. The bot must be an administrator in the chat for this to
work and must have the can_pin_messages administrator right in the
supergroup. Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.answer_callback_query
documentation:
Use this method to send answers to callback queries sent from inline
keyboards. The answer will be displayed to the user as a notification
at the top of the chat screen or as an alert. On success, True is
returned. Alternatively, the user can be redirected to the specified
Game URL. For this option to work, you must first create a game for
your bot via @BotFather and accept the terms. Otherwise, you may use
links like t.me/your_bot?start=XXXX that open your bot with a
parameter.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.get_user_chat_boosts
documentation:
Use this method to get the list of boosts added to a chat by a user.
Requires administrator rights in the chat. Returns a UserChatBoosts
object.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.get_business_connection
documentation:
Use this method to get information about the connection of the bot
with a business account. Returns a BusinessConnection object on
success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.set_my_commands
documentation:
Use this method to change the list of the bot's commands. See this
manual for more details about bot commands. Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.delete_my_commands
documentation:
Use this method to delete the list of the bot's commands for the given
scope and user language. After deletion, higher level commands will be
shown to affected users. Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.get_my_commands
documentation:
Use this method to get the current list of the bot's commands for the
given scope and user language. Returns an Array of BotCommand objects.
If commands aren't set, an empty list is returned.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.set_my_name
documentation: Use this method to change the bot's name. Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.get_my_name
documentation:
Use this method to get the current bot name for the given user
language. Returns BotName on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.set_my_description
documentation:
Use this method to change the bot's description, which is shown in the
chat with the bot if the chat is empty. Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.get_my_description
documentation:
Use this method to get the current bot description for the given user
language. Returns BotDescription on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.set_my_short_description
documentation:
Use this method to change the bot's short description, which is shown
on the bot's profile page and is sent together with the link when
users share the bot. Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.get_my_short_description
documentation:
Use this method to get the current bot short description for the given
user language. Returns BotShortDescription on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.set_chat_menu_button
documentation:
Use this method to change the bot's menu button in a private chat, or
the default menu button. Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.get_chat_menu_button
documentation:
Use this method to get the current value of the bot's menu button in a
private chat, or the default menu button. Returns MenuButton on
success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.set_my_default_administrator_rights
documentation:
Use this method to change the default administrator rights requested
by the bot when it's added as an administrator to groups or channels.
These rights will be suggested to users, but they are free to modify
the list before adding the bot. Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.get_my_default_administrator_rights
documentation:
Use this method to get the current default administrator rights of the
bot. Returns ChatAdministratorRights on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.edit_message_text
documentation:
Use this method to edit text and game messages. On success, if the
edited message is not an inline message, the edited Message is
returned, otherwise True is returned. Note that business messages that
were not sent by the bot and do not contain an inline keyboard can
only be edited within 48 hours from the time they were sent.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.edit_message_caption
documentation:
Use this method to edit captions of messages. On success, if the
edited message is not an inline message, the edited Message is
returned, otherwise True is returned. Note that business messages that
were not sent by the bot and do not contain an inline keyboard can
only be edited within 48 hours from the time they were sent.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.edit_message_media
documentation:
Use this method to edit animation, audio, document, photo, or video
messages, or to add media to text messages. If a message is part of a
message album, then it can be edited only to an audio for audio
albums, only to a document for document albums and to a photo or a
video otherwise. When an inline message is edited, a new file can't be
uploaded; use a previously uploaded file via its file_id or specify a
URL. On success, if the edited message is not an inline message, the
edited Message is returned, otherwise True is returned. Note that
business messages that were not sent by the bot and do not contain an
inline keyboard can only be edited within 48 hours from the time they
were sent.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.edit_message_live_location
documentation:
Use this method to edit live location messages. A location can be
edited until its live_period expires or editing is explicitly disabled
by a call to stopMessageLiveLocation. On success, if the edited
message is not an inline message, the edited Message is returned,
otherwise True is returned.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.stop_message_live_location
documentation:
Use this method to stop updating a live location message before
live_period expires. On success, if the message is not an inline
message, the edited Message is returned, otherwise True is returned.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.edit_message_reply_markup
documentation:
Use this method to edit only the reply markup of messages. On success,
if the edited message is not an inline message, the edited Message is
returned, otherwise True is returned. Note that business messages that
were not sent by the bot and do not contain an inline keyboard can
only be edited within 48 hours from the time they were sent.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.stop_poll
documentation:
Use this method to stop a poll which was sent by the bot. On success,
the stopped Poll is returned.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.delete_message
documentation:
Use this method to delete a message, including service messages, with
the following limitations: - A message can only be deleted if it was
sent less than 48 hours ago. - Service messages about a supergroup,
channel, or forum topic creation can't be deleted. - A dice message in
a private chat can only be deleted if it was sent more than 24 hours
ago. - Bots can delete outgoing messages in private chats, groups, and
supergroups. - Bots can delete incoming messages in private chats. -
Bots granted can_post_messages permissions can delete outgoing
messages in channels. - If the bot is an administrator of a group, it
can delete any message there. - If the bot has can_delete_messages
permission in a supergroup or a channel, it can delete any message
there. Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.delete_messages
documentation:
Use this method to delete multiple messages simultaneously. If some of
the specified messages can't be found, they are skipped. Returns True
on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.send_sticker
documentation:
Use this method to send static .WEBP, animated .TGS, or video .WEBM
stickers. On success, the sent Message is returned.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.get_sticker_set
documentation:
Use this method to get a sticker set. On success, a StickerSet object
is returned.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.get_custom_emoji_stickers
documentation:
Use this method to get information about custom emoji stickers by
their identifiers. Returns an Array of Sticker objects.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.upload_sticker_file
documentation:
Use this method to upload a file with a sticker for later use in the
createNewStickerSet, addStickerToSet, or replaceStickerInSet methods
(the file can be used multiple times). Returns the uploaded File on
success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.create_new_sticker_set
documentation:
Use this method to create a new sticker set owned by a user. The bot
will be able to edit the sticker set thus created. Returns True on
success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.add_sticker_to_set
documentation:
Use this method to add a new sticker to a set created by the bot.
Emoji sticker sets can have up to 200 stickers. Other sticker sets can
have up to 120 stickers. Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.set_sticker_position_in_set
documentation:
Use this method to move a sticker in a set created by the bot to a
specific position. Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.delete_sticker_from_set
documentation:
Use this method to delete a sticker from a set created by the bot.
Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.replace_sticker_in_set
documentation:
Use this method to replace an existing sticker in a sticker set with a
new one. The method is equivalent to calling deleteStickerFromSet,
then addStickerToSet, then setStickerPositionInSet. Returns True on
success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.set_sticker_emoji_list
documentation:
Use this method to change the list of emoji assigned to a regular or
custom emoji sticker. The sticker must belong to a sticker set created
by the bot. Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.set_sticker_keywords
documentation:
Use this method to change search keywords assigned to a regular or
custom emoji sticker. The sticker must belong to a sticker set created
by the bot. Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.set_sticker_mask_position
documentation:
Use this method to change the mask position of a mask sticker. The
sticker must belong to a sticker set that was created by the bot.
Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.set_sticker_set_title
documentation:
Use this method to set the title of a created sticker set. Returns
True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.set_sticker_set_thumbnail
documentation:
Use this method to set the thumbnail of a regular or mask sticker set.
The format of the thumbnail file must match the format of the stickers
in the set. Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.set_custom_emoji_sticker_set_thumbnail
documentation:
Use this method to set the thumbnail of a custom emoji sticker set.
Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.delete_sticker_set
documentation:
Use this method to delete a sticker set that was created by the bot.
Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.get_available_gifts
documentation:
Returns the list of gifts that can be sent by the bot to users.
Requires no parameters. Returns a Gifts object.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.send_gift
documentation:
Sends a gift to the given user. The gift can't be converted to
Telegram Stars by the user. Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.verify_user
documentation:
Verifies a user on behalf of the organization which is represented by
the bot. Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.verify_chat
documentation:
Verifies a chat on behalf of the organization which is represented by
the bot. Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.remove_user_verification
documentation:
Removes verification from a user who is currently verified on behalf
of the organization represented by the bot. Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.remove_chat_verification
documentation:
Removes verification from a chat that is currently verified on behalf
of the organization represented by the bot. Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.answer_inline_query
documentation:
Use this method to send answers to an inline query. On success, True
is returned. No more than 50 results per query are allowed.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.answer_web_app_query
documentation:
Use this method to set the result of an interaction with a Web App and
send a corresponding message on behalf of the user to the chat from
which the query originated. On success, a SentWebAppMessage object is
returned.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.save_prepared_inline_message
documentation:
Stores a message that can be sent by a user of a Mini App. Returns a
PreparedInlineMessage object.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.send_invoice
documentation:
Use this method to send invoices. On success, the sent Message is
returned.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.create_invoice_link
documentation:
Use this method to create a link for an invoice. Returns the created
invoice link as String on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.answer_shipping_query
documentation:
If you sent an invoice requesting a shipping address and the parameter
is_flexible was specified, the Bot API will send an Update with a
shipping_query field to the bot. Use this method to reply to shipping
queries. On success, True is returned.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.answer_pre_checkout_query
documentation:
Once the user has confirmed their payment and shipping details, the
Bot API sends the final confirmation in the form of an Update with the
field pre_checkout_query. Use this method to respond to such pre-
checkout queries. On success, True is returned. Note: The Bot API must
receive an answer within 10 seconds after the pre-checkout query was
sent.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.get_star_transactions
documentation:
Returns the bot's Telegram Star transactions in chronological order.
On success, returns a StarTransactions object.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.refund_star_payment
documentation:
Refunds a successful payment in Telegram Stars. Returns True on
success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.edit_user_star_subscription
documentation:
Allows the bot to cancel or re-enable extension of a subscription paid
in Telegram Stars. Returns True on success.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.set_passport_data_errors
documentation:
Informs a user that some of the Telegram Passport elements they
provided contains errors. The user will not be able to re-submit their
Passport to you until the errors are fixed (the contents of the field
for which you returned the error must change). Returns True on
success. Use this if the data submitted by the user doesn't satisfy
the standards your service requires for any reason. For example, if a
birthday date seems invalid, a submitted document is blurry, a scan
shows evidence of tampering, etc. Supply some details in the error
message to make sure the user knows how to correct the issues.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.send_game
documentation:
Use this method to send a game. On success, the sent Message is
returned.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.set_game_score
documentation:
Use this method to set the score of the specified user in a game
message. On success, if the message is not an inline message, the
Message is returned, otherwise True is returned. Returns an error, if
the new score is not greater than the user's current score in the chat
and force is False.

methods.name: wonda.types.methods.APIMethods.get_game_high_scores
documentation:
Use this method to get data for high score tables. Will return the
score of the specified user and several of their neighbors in a game.
Returns an Array of GameHighScore objects. This method will currently
return scores for the target user, plus two of their closest neighbors
on each side. Will also return the top three users if the user and
their neighbors are not among them. Please note that this behavior is
subject to change.

classes.name: wonda.tools.localization.abc.ABCLocalizator

methods.name: wonda.tools.localization.abc.ABCLocalizator.get_locale
documentation: Retrieves a locale via its language code.

methods.name: wonda.tools.localization.abc.ABCLocalizator.load_translations
documentation:
Loads translations from a specified path. Controls how translations
are loaded from a given directory.

Defines the interface for localization, like retrieving locales 
and loading translations.

classes.name: wonda.tools.keyboard.builders.reply.ReplyKeyboardBuilder

methods.name: wonda.tools.keyboard.builders.reply.ReplyKeyboardBuilder.remove
documentation: A shortcut to remove a reply keyboard.
A builder for a keyboard with custom reply options.

classes.name: wonda.tools.keyboard.builders.inline.InlineKeyboardBuilder
documentation:
An inline keyboard builder. No options are available
for this type of keyboard.

classes.name: wonda.tools.keyboard.builders.inline.PaginatedInlineKeyboardBuilder
documentation: A paginated inline keyboard builder.

classes.name: wonda.tools.keyboard.base.BaseKeyboardBuilder

methods.name: wonda.tools.keyboard.base.BaseKeyboardBuilder.keyboard
documentation: Keyboard markup to be sent.

methods.name: wonda.tools.keyboard.base.BaseKeyboardBuilder.last_row
documentation: Convenience property to get the last button row.

A basic keyboard builder which implements independent logic for
`.add()` and `.row()` methods

classes.name: wonda.tools.keyboard.abc.ABCKeyboardBuilder

methods.name: wonda.tools.keyboard.abc.ABCKeyboardBuilder.add
documentation: Adds a button to the keyboard.

methods.name: wonda.tools.keyboard.abc.ABCKeyboardBuilder.row
documentation: Adds a row to the keyboard. Panics if the last row was empty.

methods.name: wonda.tools.keyboard.abc.ABCKeyboardBuilder.build
documentation: Builds the keyboard.
A keyboard builder interface.

methods.name: wonda.tools.keyboard.abc.ABCButton.dict
documentation: Returns the button data.

functions.name: wonda.tools.web_apps.verify_webapp_request
documentation: Validates that the update is from Telegram.

functions.name: wonda.tools.web_apps.validate_webapp_data
documentation: Verifies the authenticity of the data by computing the hash of the request.

classes.name: wonda.tools.keyboard.elements.Button

methods.name: wonda.tools.keyboard.elements.Button.dict
documentation: Returns the button data.
A basic text button.

classes.name: wonda.tools.keyboard.elements.App
documentation:
This button opens specified web app which will be able to send an arbitrary message
on behalf of the user using the method `answerWebAppQuery`. Available only
in private chats between a user and the bot.

classes.name: wonda.tools.keyboard.elements.Callback
documentation: A button that sends a callback query to the bot when pressed.

classes.name: wonda.tools.keyboard.elements.Game
documentation:
This button will launch a game in the chat when pressed.
This type of button must always be the first button in the first row.

classes.name: wonda.tools.keyboard.elements.Login
documentation:
A button containing a login URL. This button will open a web page
with the specified URL and ask the user to log in. Can be used
as a replacement for the Telegram Login Widget.

classes.name: wonda.tools.keyboard.elements.Pay
documentation:
A pay button. This type of button must always be the first button
in the first row and can only be used in invoice messages.

classes.name: wonda.tools.keyboard.elements.Switch
documentation:
Pressing the button will open specified inline query in the chat,
either current or selected by the user.

classes.name: wonda.tools.keyboard.elements.URL
documentation: When this button is pressed, HTTP or tg:// link is opened.

classes.name: wonda.tools.keyboard.elements.Contact
documentation:
When pressed, the user's phone number will be sent as a contact.
Available in private chats only.

classes.name: wonda.tools.keyboard.elements.Location
documentation:
When pressed, the user's current location will be sent.
Available in private chats only.

classes.name: wonda.tools.keyboard.elements.Poll
documentation:
When pressed, the user will be asked to create a poll of specified type
and send it to the bot. Available in private chats only.

classes.name: wonda.tools.keyboard.elements.RequestUsers
documentation:
Pressing the button will open a list of users suitable for given criteria.
Tapping on any user will send their identifier to the bot in a "user_shared"
service message. Available in private chats only.

classes.name: wonda.tools.keyboard.elements.RequestChat
documentation:
Pressing the button will open a list of chats suitable for given criteria.
Tapping on any chat will send its identifier to the bot in a "chat_shared"
service message. Available in private chats only.

classes.name: wonda.contrib.middleware.rate_limiting.RateLimitingMiddleware
documentation: Limits how often your bot should handle messages from a user.

classes.name: wonda.contrib.middleware.internationalization.InternationalizationMiddleware
documentation: Provides foreign language capabilities for your bot.

variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InputFile
documentation: This object represents the contents of a file to be uploaded. Must be posted using multipart/form-data in the usual way that files are uploaded via the browser.

variables.name: wonda.types.objects.MessageOrigin
documentation:
This object describes the origin of a message. It can be one of
MessageOriginUser, MessageOriginHiddenUser, MessageOriginChat,
MessageOriginChannel.

variables.name: wonda.types.objects.PaidMedia
documentation:
This object describes paid media. Currently, it can be one of
PaidMediaPreview, PaidMediaPhoto, PaidMediaVideo.

variables.name: wonda.types.objects.BackgroundFill
documentation:
This object describes the way a background is filled based on the
selected colors. Currently, it can be one of BackgroundFillSolid,
BackgroundFillGradient, BackgroundFillFreeformGradient.

variables.name: wonda.types.objects.BackgroundType
documentation:
This object describes the type of a background. Currently, it can be
one of BackgroundTypeFill, BackgroundTypeWallpaper,
BackgroundTypePattern, BackgroundTypeChatTheme.

variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatMember
documentation:
This object contains information about one member of a chat.
Currently, the following 6 types of chat members are supported:
ChatMemberOwner, ChatMemberAdministrator, ChatMemberMember,
ChatMemberRestricted, ChatMemberLeft, ChatMemberBanned.

variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ReactionType
documentation:
This object describes the type of a reaction. Currently, it can be one
of ReactionTypeEmoji, ReactionTypeCustomEmoji, ReactionTypePaid.

variables.name: wonda.types.objects.BotCommandScope
documentation:
This object represents the scope to which bot commands are applied.
Currently, the following 7 scopes are supported:
BotCommandScopeDefault, BotCommandScopeAllPrivateChats,
BotCommandScopeAllGroupChats, BotCommandScopeAllChatAdministrators,
BotCommandScopeChat, BotCommandScopeChatAdministrators,
BotCommandScopeChatMember.

variables.name: wonda.types.objects.MenuButton
documentation:
This object describes the bot's menu button in a private chat. It
should be one of MenuButtonCommands, MenuButtonWebApp,
MenuButtonDefault.

variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatBoostSource
documentation:
This object describes the source of a chat boost. It can be one of
ChatBoostSourcePremium, ChatBoostSourceGiftCode,
ChatBoostSourceGiveaway.

variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InputMedia
documentation:
This object represents the content of a media message to be sent. It
should be one of InputMediaAnimation, InputMediaDocument,
InputMediaAudio, InputMediaPhoto, InputMediaVideo.

variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InputPaidMedia
documentation:
This object describes the paid media to be sent. Currently, it can be
one of InputPaidMediaPhoto, InputPaidMediaVideo.

variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InlineQueryResult
documentation:
This object represents one result of an inline query. Telegram clients
currently support results of the following 20 types:
InlineQueryResultCachedAudio, InlineQueryResultCachedDocument,
InlineQueryResultCachedGif, InlineQueryResultCachedMpeg4Gif,
InlineQueryResultCachedPhoto, InlineQueryResultCachedSticker,
InlineQueryResultCachedVideo, InlineQueryResultCachedVoice,
InlineQueryResultArticle, InlineQueryResultAudio,
InlineQueryResultContact, InlineQueryResultGame,
InlineQueryResultDocument, InlineQueryResultGif,
InlineQueryResultLocation, InlineQueryResultMpeg4Gif,
InlineQueryResultPhoto, InlineQueryResultVenue,
InlineQueryResultVideo, InlineQueryResultVoice.

variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InputMessageContent
documentation:
This object represents the content of a message to be sent as a result
of an inline query. Telegram clients currently support the following 5
types: InputTextMessageContent, InputLocationMessageContent,
InputVenueMessageContent, InputContactMessageContent,
InputInvoiceMessageContent.

variables.name: wonda.types.objects.RevenueWithdrawalState
documentation:
This object describes the state of a revenue withdrawal operation.
Currently, it can be one of RevenueWithdrawalStatePending,
RevenueWithdrawalStateSucceeded, RevenueWithdrawalStateFailed.

variables.name: wonda.types.objects.TransactionPartner
documentation:
This object describes the source of a transaction, or its recipient
for outgoing transactions. Currently, it can be one of
TransactionPartnerUser, TransactionPartnerAffiliateProgram,
TransactionPartnerFragment, TransactionPartnerTelegramAds,
TransactionPartnerTelegramApi, TransactionPartnerOther.

variables.name: wonda.types.objects.PassportElementError
documentation:
This object represents an error in the Telegram Passport element which
was submitted that should be resolved by the user. It should be one
of: PassportElementErrorDataField, PassportElementErrorFrontSide,
PassportElementErrorReverseSide, PassportElementErrorSelfie,
PassportElementErrorFile, PassportElementErrorFiles,
PassportElementErrorTranslationFile,
PassportElementErrorTranslationFiles, PassportElementErrorUnspecified.

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.Update

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Update.update_id
documentation:
The update's unique identifier. Update identifiers start from a
certain positive number and increase sequentially. This identifier
becomes especially handy if you're using webhooks, since it allows you
to ignore repeated updates or to restore the correct update sequence,
should they get out of order. If there are no new updates for at least
a week, then identifier of the next update will be chosen randomly
instead of sequentially.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Update.message
documentation:
Optional. New incoming message of any kind - text, photo, sticker,
etc.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Update.edited_message
documentation:
Optional. New version of a message that is known to the bot and was
edited. This update may at times be triggered by changes to message
fields that are either unavailable or not actively used by your bot.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Update.channel_post
documentation:
Optional. New incoming channel post of any kind - text, photo,
sticker, etc.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Update.edited_channel_post
documentation:
Optional. New version of a channel post that is known to the bot and
was edited. This update may at times be triggered by changes to
message fields that are either unavailable or not actively used by
your bot.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Update.business_connection
documentation:
Optional. The bot was connected to or disconnected from a business
account, or a user edited an existing connection with the bot

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Update.business_message
documentation: Optional. New message from a connected business account

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Update.edited_business_message
documentation: Optional. New version of a message from a connected business account

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Update.deleted_business_messages
documentation: Optional. Messages were deleted from a connected business account

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Update.message_reaction
documentation:
Optional. A reaction to a message was changed by a user. The bot must
be an administrator in the chat and must explicitly specify
"message_reaction" in the list of allowed_updates to receive these
updates. The update isn't received for reactions set by bots.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Update.message_reaction_count
documentation:
Optional. Reactions to a message with anonymous reactions were
changed. The bot must be an administrator in the chat and must
explicitly specify "message_reaction_count" in the list of
allowed_updates to receive these updates. The updates are grouped and
can be sent with delay up to a few minutes.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Update.inline_query
documentation: Optional. New incoming inline query

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Update.chosen_inline_result
documentation:
Optional. The result of an inline query that was chosen by a user and
sent to their chat partner. Please see our documentation on the
feedback collecting for details on how to enable these updates for
your bot.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Update.callback_query
documentation: Optional. New incoming callback query

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Update.shipping_query
documentation:
Optional. New incoming shipping query. Only for invoices with flexible
price

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Update.pre_checkout_query
documentation:
Optional. New incoming pre-checkout query. Contains full information
about checkout

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Update.purchased_paid_media
documentation:
Optional. A user purchased paid media with a non-empty payload sent by
the bot in a non-channel chat

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Update.poll
documentation:
Optional. New poll state. Bots receive only updates about manually
stopped polls and polls, which are sent by the bot

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Update.poll_answer
documentation:
Optional. A user changed their answer in a non-anonymous poll. Bots
receive new votes only in polls that were sent by the bot itself.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Update.my_chat_member
documentation:
Optional. The bot's chat member status was updated in a chat. For
private chats, this update is received only when the bot is blocked or
unblocked by the user.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Update.chat_member
documentation:
Optional. A chat member's status was updated in a chat. The bot must
be an administrator in the chat and must explicitly specify
"chat_member" in the list of allowed_updates to receive these updates.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Update.chat_join_request
documentation:
Optional. A request to join the chat has been sent. The bot must have
the can_invite_users administrator right in the chat to receive these
updates.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Update.chat_boost
documentation:
Optional. A chat boost was added or changed. The bot must be an
administrator in the chat to receive these updates.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Update.removed_chat_boost
documentation:
Optional. A boost was removed from a chat. The bot must be an
administrator in the chat to receive these updates.

This object represents an incoming update. At most one of the optional
parameters can be present in any given update.

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.WebhookInfo

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.WebhookInfo.url
documentation: Webhook URL, may be empty if webhook is not set up

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.WebhookInfo.has_custom_certificate
documentation:
True, if a custom certificate was provided for webhook certificate
checks

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.WebhookInfo.pending_update_count
documentation: Number of updates awaiting delivery

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.WebhookInfo.ip_address
documentation: Optional. Currently used webhook IP address

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.WebhookInfo.last_error_date
documentation:
Optional. Unix time for the most recent error that happened when
trying to deliver an update via webhook

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.WebhookInfo.last_error_message
documentation:
Optional. Error message in human-readable format for the most recent
error that happened when trying to deliver an update via webhook

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.WebhookInfo.last_synchronization_error_date
documentation:
Optional. Unix time of the most recent error that happened when trying
to synchronize available updates with Telegram datacenters

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.WebhookInfo.max_connections
documentation:
Optional. The maximum allowed number of simultaneous HTTPS connections
to the webhook for update delivery

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.WebhookInfo.allowed_updates
documentation:
Optional. A list of update types the bot is subscribed to. Defaults to
all update types except chat_member
Describes the current status of a webhook.

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.User

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.User.id
documentation:
Unique identifier for this user or bot. This number may have more than
32 significant bits and some programming languages may have
difficulty/silent defects in interpreting it. But it has at most 52
significant bits, so a 64-bit integer or double-precision float type
are safe for storing this identifier.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.User.is_bot
documentation: True, if this user is a bot

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.User.first_name
documentation: User's or bot's first name

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.User.last_name
documentation: Optional. User's or bot's last name

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.User.username
documentation: Optional. User's or bot's username

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.User.language_code
documentation: Optional. IETF language tag of the user's language

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.User.can_join_groups
documentation:
Optional. True, if the bot can be invited to groups. Returned only in
getMe.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.User.can_read_all_group_messages
documentation:
Optional. True, if privacy mode is disabled for the bot. Returned only
in getMe.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.User.supports_inline_queries
documentation:
Optional. True, if the bot supports inline queries. Returned only in
getMe.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.User.can_connect_to_business
documentation:
Optional. True, if the bot can be connected to a Telegram Business
account to receive its messages. Returned only in getMe.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.User.has_main_web_app
documentation: Optional. True, if the bot has a main Web App. Returned only in getMe.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.User.is_premium
documentation: Optional. True, if this user is a Telegram Premium user

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.User.added_to_attachment_menu
documentation: Optional. True, if this user added the bot to the attachment menu
This object represents a Telegram user or bot.

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.Chat

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Chat.id
documentation:
Unique identifier for this chat. This number may have more than 32
significant bits and some programming languages may have
difficulty/silent defects in interpreting it. But it has at most 52
significant bits, so a signed 64-bit integer or double-precision float
type are safe for storing this identifier.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Chat.type
documentation:
Type of the chat, can be either "private", "group", "supergroup" or
"channel"

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Chat.title
documentation: Optional. Title, for supergroups, channels and group chats

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Chat.username
documentation:
Optional. Username, for private chats, supergroups and channels if
available

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Chat.first_name
documentation: Optional. First name of the other party in a private chat

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Chat.last_name
documentation: Optional. Last name of the other party in a private chat

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Chat.is_forum
documentation: Optional. True, if the supergroup chat is a forum (has topics enabled)
This object represents a chat.

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.id
documentation:
Unique identifier for this chat. This number may have more than 32
significant bits and some programming languages may have
difficulty/silent defects in interpreting it. But it has at most 52
significant bits, so a signed 64-bit integer or double-precision float
type are safe for storing this identifier.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.type
documentation:
Type of the chat, can be either "private", "group", "supergroup" or
"channel"

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.accent_color_id
documentation:
Identifier of the accent color for the chat name and backgrounds of
the chat photo, reply header, and link preview. See accent colors for
more details.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.max_reaction_count
documentation:
The maximum number of reactions that can be set on a message in the
chat

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.title
documentation: Optional. Title, for supergroups, channels and group chats

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.username
documentation:
Optional. Username, for private chats, supergroups and channels if
available

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.first_name
documentation: Optional. First name of the other party in a private chat

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.last_name
documentation: Optional. Last name of the other party in a private chat

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.photo
documentation: Optional. Chat photo

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.active_usernames
documentation:
Optional. If non-empty, the list of all active chat usernames; for
private chats, supergroups and channels

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.birthdate
documentation: Optional. For private chats, the date of birth of the user

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.business_intro
documentation:
Optional. For private chats with business accounts, the intro of the
business

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.business_location
documentation:
Optional. For private chats with business accounts, the location of
the business

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.business_opening_hours
documentation:
Optional. For private chats with business accounts, the opening hours
of the business

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.personal_chat
documentation: Optional. For private chats, the personal channel of the user

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.available_reactions
documentation:
Optional. List of available reactions allowed in the chat. If omitted,
then all emoji reactions are allowed.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.background_custom_emoji_id
documentation:
Optional. Custom emoji identifier of the emoji chosen by the chat for
the reply header and link preview background

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.profile_accent_color_id
documentation:
Optional. Identifier of the accent color for the chat's profile
background. See profile accent colors for more details.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.profile_background_custom_emoji_id
documentation:
Optional. Custom emoji identifier of the emoji chosen by the chat for
its profile background

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.emoji_status_custom_emoji_id
documentation:
Optional. Custom emoji identifier of the emoji status of the chat or
the other party in a private chat

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.emoji_status_expiration_date
documentation:
Optional. Expiration date of the emoji status of the chat or the other
party in a private chat, in Unix time, if any

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.bio
documentation: Optional. Bio of the other party in a private chat

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.description
documentation: Optional. Description, for groups, supergroups and channel chats

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.invite_link
documentation:
Optional. Primary invite link, for groups, supergroups and channel
chats

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.pinned_message
documentation: Optional. The most recent pinned message (by sending date)

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.permissions
documentation: Optional. Default chat member permissions, for groups and supergroups

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.slow_mode_delay
documentation:
Optional. For supergroups, the minimum allowed delay between
consecutive messages sent by each unprivileged user; in seconds

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.unrestrict_boost_count
documentation:
Optional. For supergroups, the minimum number of boosts that a non-
administrator user needs to add in order to ignore slow mode and chat
permissions

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.message_auto_delete_time
documentation:
Optional. The time after which all messages sent to the chat will be
automatically deleted; in seconds

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.sticker_set_name
documentation: Optional. For supergroups, name of the group sticker set

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.custom_emoji_sticker_set_name
documentation:
Optional. For supergroups, the name of the group's custom emoji
sticker set. Custom emoji from this set can be used by all users and
bots in the group.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.linked_chat_id
documentation:
Optional. Unique identifier for the linked chat, i.e. the discussion
group identifier for a channel and vice versa; for supergroups and
channel chats. This identifier may be greater than 32 bits and some
programming languages may have difficulty/silent defects in
interpreting it. But it is smaller than 52 bits, so a signed 64 bit
integer or double-precision float type are safe for storing this
identifier.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.location
documentation:
Optional. For supergroups, the location to which the supergroup is
connected

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.is_forum
documentation: Optional. True, if the supergroup chat is a forum (has topics enabled)

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.has_private_forwards
documentation:
Optional. True, if privacy settings of the other party in the private
chat allows to use tg://user?id=<user_id> links only in chats with the
user

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.has_restricted_voice_and_video_messages
documentation:
Optional. True, if the privacy settings of the other party restrict
sending voice and video note messages in the private chat

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.join_to_send_messages
documentation:
Optional. True, if users need to join the supergroup before they can
send messages

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.join_by_request
documentation:
Optional. True, if all users directly joining the supergroup without
using an invite link need to be approved by supergroup administrators

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.can_send_paid_media
documentation:
Optional. True, if paid media messages can be sent or forwarded to the
channel chat. The field is available only for channel chats.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.has_aggressive_anti_spam_enabled
documentation:
