Optional. True, if aggressive anti-spam checks are enabled in the
supergroup. The field is only available to chat administrators.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.has_hidden_members
documentation:
Optional. True, if non-administrators can only get the list of bots
and administrators in the chat

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.has_protected_content
documentation:
Optional. True, if messages from the chat can't be forwarded to other
chats

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.has_visible_history
documentation:
Optional. True, if new chat members will have access to old messages;
available only to chat administrators

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatFullInfo.can_set_sticker_set
documentation: Optional. True, if the bot can change the group sticker set
This object contains full information about a chat.

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.Message

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.message_id
documentation:
Unique message identifier inside this chat. In specific instances
(e.g., message containing a video sent to a big chat), the server
might automatically schedule a message instead of sending it
immediately. In such cases, this field will be 0 and the relevant
message will be unusable until it is actually sent

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.date
documentation:
Date the message was sent in Unix time. It is always a positive
number, representing a valid date.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.chat
documentation: Chat the message belongs to

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.message_thread_id
documentation:
Optional. Unique identifier of a message thread to which the message
belongs; for supergroups only

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.from_
documentation:
Optional. Sender of the message; may be empty for messages sent to
channels. For backward compatibility, if the message was sent on
behalf of a chat, the field contains a fake sender user in non-channel
chats

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.sender_chat
documentation:
Optional. Sender of the message when sent on behalf of a chat. For
example, the supergroup itself for messages sent by its anonymous
administrators or a linked channel for messages automatically
forwarded to the channel's discussion group. For backward
compatibility, if the message was sent on behalf of a chat, the field
from contains a fake sender user in non-channel chats.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.sender_boost_count
documentation:
Optional. If the sender of the message boosted the chat, the number of
boosts added by the user

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.sender_business_bot
documentation:
Optional. The bot that actually sent the message on behalf of the
business account. Available only for outgoing messages sent on behalf
of the connected business account.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.business_connection_id
documentation:
Optional. Unique identifier of the business connection from which the
message was received. If non-empty, the message belongs to a chat of
the corresponding business account that is independent from any
potential bot chat which might share the same identifier.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.forward_origin
documentation:
Optional. Information about the original message for forwarded
messages

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.reply_to_message
documentation:
Optional. For replies in the same chat and message thread, the
original message. Note that the Message object in this field will not
contain further reply_to_message fields even if it itself is a reply.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.external_reply
documentation:
Optional. Information about the message that is being replied to,
which may come from another chat or forum topic

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.quote
documentation:
Optional. For replies that quote part of the original message, the
quoted part of the message

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.reply_to_story
documentation: Optional. For replies to a story, the original story

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.via_bot
documentation: Optional. Bot through which the message was sent

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.edit_date
documentation: Optional. Date the message was last edited in Unix time

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.media_group_id
documentation:
Optional. The unique identifier of a media message group this message
belongs to

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.author_signature
documentation:
Optional. Signature of the post author for messages in channels, or
the custom title of an anonymous group administrator

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.text
documentation: Optional. For text messages, the actual UTF-8 text of the message

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.entities
documentation:
Optional. For text messages, special entities like usernames, URLs,
bot commands, etc. that appear in the text

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.link_preview_options
documentation:
Optional. Options used for link preview generation for the message, if
it is a text message and link preview options were changed

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.effect_id
documentation: Optional. Unique identifier of the message effect added to the message

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.animation
documentation:
Optional. Message is an animation, information about the animation.
For backward compatibility, when this field is set, the document field
will also be set

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.audio
documentation: Optional. Message is an audio file, information about the file

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.document
documentation: Optional. Message is a general file, information about the file

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.paid_media
documentation:
Optional. Message contains paid media; information about the paid
media

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.photo
documentation: Optional. Message is a photo, available sizes of the photo

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.sticker
documentation: Optional. Message is a sticker, information about the sticker

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.story
documentation: Optional. Message is a forwarded story

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.video
documentation: Optional. Message is a video, information about the video

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.video_note
documentation: Optional. Message is a video note, information about the video message

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.voice
documentation: Optional. Message is a voice message, information about the file

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.caption
documentation:
Optional. Caption for the animation, audio, document, paid media,
photo, video or voice

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.caption_entities
documentation:
Optional. For messages with a caption, special entities like
usernames, URLs, bot commands, etc. that appear in the caption

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.contact
documentation: Optional. Message is a shared contact, information about the contact

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.dice
documentation: Optional. Message is a dice with random value

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.game
documentation:
Optional. Message is a game, information about the game. More about
games: https://core.telegram.org/bots/api/#games

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.poll
documentation: Optional. Message is a native poll, information about the poll

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.venue
documentation:
Optional. Message is a venue, information about the venue. For
backward compatibility, when this field is set, the location field
will also be set

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.location
documentation: Optional. Message is a shared location, information about the location

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.new_chat_members
documentation:
Optional. New members that were added to the group or supergroup and
information about them (the bot itself may be one of these members)

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.left_chat_member
documentation:
Optional. A member was removed from the group, information about them
(this member may be the bot itself)

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.new_chat_title
documentation: Optional. A chat title was changed to this value

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.new_chat_photo
documentation: Optional. A chat photo was change to this value

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.message_auto_delete_timer_changed
documentation:
Optional. Service message: auto-delete timer settings changed in the
chat

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.migrate_to_chat_id
documentation:
Optional. The group has been migrated to a supergroup with the
specified identifier. This number may have more than 32 significant
bits and some programming languages may have difficulty/silent defects
in interpreting it. But it has at most 52 significant bits, so a
signed 64-bit integer or double-precision float type are safe for
storing this identifier.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.migrate_from_chat_id
documentation:
Optional. The supergroup has been migrated from a group with the
specified identifier. This number may have more than 32 significant
bits and some programming languages may have difficulty/silent defects
in interpreting it. But it has at most 52 significant bits, so a
signed 64-bit integer or double-precision float type are safe for
storing this identifier.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.pinned_message
documentation:
Optional. Specified message was pinned. Note that the Message object
in this field will not contain further reply_to_message fields even if
it itself is a reply.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.invoice
documentation:
Optional. Message is an invoice for a payment, information about the
invoice. More about payments:
https://core.telegram.org/bots/api/#payments

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.successful_payment
documentation:
Optional. Message is a service message about a successful payment,
information about the payment. More about payments:
https://core.telegram.org/bots/api/#payments

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.refunded_payment
documentation:
Optional. Message is a service message about a refunded payment,
information about the payment. More about payments:
https://core.telegram.org/bots/api/#payments

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.users_shared
documentation: Optional. Service message: users were shared with the bot

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.chat_shared
documentation: Optional. Service message: a chat was shared with the bot

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.connected_website
documentation:
Optional. The domain name of the website on which the user has logged
in. More about Telegram Login: https://core.telegram.org/widgets/login

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.write_access_allowed
documentation:
Optional. Service message: the user allowed the bot to write messages
after adding it to the attachment or side menu, launching a Web App
from a link, or accepting an explicit request from a Web App sent by
the method requestWriteAccess

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.passport_data
documentation: Optional. Telegram Passport data

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.proximity_alert_triggered
documentation:
Optional. Service message. A user in the chat triggered another user's
proximity alert while sharing Live Location.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.boost_added
documentation: Optional. Service message: user boosted the chat

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.chat_background_set
documentation: Optional. Service message: chat background set

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.forum_topic_created
documentation: Optional. Service message: forum topic created

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.forum_topic_edited
documentation: Optional. Service message: forum topic edited

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.forum_topic_closed
documentation: Optional. Service message: forum topic closed

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.forum_topic_reopened
documentation: Optional. Service message: forum topic reopened

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.general_forum_topic_hidden
documentation: Optional. Service message: the 'General' forum topic hidden

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.general_forum_topic_unhidden
documentation: Optional. Service message: the 'General' forum topic unhidden

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.giveaway_created
documentation: Optional. Service message: a scheduled giveaway was created

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.giveaway
documentation: Optional. The message is a scheduled giveaway message

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.giveaway_winners
documentation: Optional. A giveaway with public winners was completed

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.giveaway_completed
documentation:
Optional. Service message: a giveaway without public winners was
completed

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.video_chat_scheduled
documentation: Optional. Service message: video chat scheduled

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.video_chat_started
documentation: Optional. Service message: video chat started

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.video_chat_ended
documentation: Optional. Service message: video chat ended

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.video_chat_participants_invited
documentation: Optional. Service message: new participants invited to a video chat

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.web_app_data
documentation: Optional. Service message: data sent by a Web App

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.reply_markup
documentation:
Optional. Inline keyboard attached to the message. login_url buttons
are represented as ordinary url buttons.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.is_topic_message
documentation: Optional. True, if the message is sent to a forum topic

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.is_automatic_forward
documentation:
Optional. True, if the message is a channel post that was
automatically forwarded to the connected discussion group

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.has_protected_content
documentation: Optional. True, if the message can't be forwarded

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.is_from_offline
documentation:
Optional. True, if the message was sent by an implicit action, for
example, as an away or a greeting business message, or as a scheduled
message

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.show_caption_above_media
documentation: Optional. True, if the caption must be shown above the message media

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.has_media_spoiler
documentation: Optional. True, if the message media is covered by a spoiler animation

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.delete_chat_photo
documentation: Optional. Service message: the chat photo was deleted

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.group_chat_created
documentation: Optional. Service message: the group has been created

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.supergroup_chat_created
documentation:
Optional. Service message: the supergroup has been created. This field
can't be received in a message coming through updates, because bot
can't be a member of a supergroup when it is created. It can only be
found in reply_to_message if someone replies to a very first message
in a directly created supergroup.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Message.channel_chat_created
documentation:
Optional. Service message: the channel has been created. This field
can't be received in a message coming through updates, because bot
can't be a member of a channel when it is created. It can only be
found in reply_to_message if someone replies to a very first message
in a channel.
This object represents a message.

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.MessageId

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.MessageId.message_id
documentation:
Unique message identifier. In specific instances (e.g., message
containing a video sent to a big chat), the server might automatically
schedule a message instead of sending it immediately. In such cases,
this field will be 0 and the relevant message will be unusable until
it is actually sent
This object represents a unique message identifier.

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.MessageEntity

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.MessageEntity.type
documentation:
Type of the entity. Currently, can be "mention" (@username), "hashtag"
(#hashtag or #hashtag@chatusername), "cashtag" ($USD or
$USD@chatusername), "bot_command" (/start@jobs_bot), "url"
(https://telegram.org), "email" (do-not-reply@telegram.org),
"phone_number" (+1-212-555-0123), "bold" (bold text), "italic" (italic
text), "underline" (underlined text), "strikethrough" (strikethrough
text), "spoiler" (spoiler message), "blockquote" (block quotation),
"expandable_blockquote" (collapsed-by-default block quotation), "code"
(monowidth string), "pre" (monowidth block), "text_link" (for
clickable text URLs), "text_mention" (for users without usernames),
"custom_emoji" (for inline custom emoji stickers)

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.MessageEntity.offset
documentation: Offset in UTF-16 code units to the start of the entity

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.MessageEntity.length
documentation: Length of the entity in UTF-16 code units

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.MessageEntity.url
documentation:
Optional. For "text_link" only, URL that will be opened after user
taps on the text

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.MessageEntity.user
documentation: Optional. For "text_mention" only, the mentioned user

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.MessageEntity.language
documentation: Optional. For "pre" only, the programming language of the entity text

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.MessageEntity.custom_emoji_id
documentation:
Optional. For "custom_emoji" only, unique identifier of the custom
emoji. Use getCustomEmojiStickers to get full information about the
sticker

This object represents one special entity in a text message. For
example, hashtags, usernames, URLs, etc.

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.TextQuote

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.TextQuote.text
documentation:
Text of the quoted part of a message that is replied to by the given
message

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.TextQuote.position
documentation:
Approximate quote position in the original message in UTF-16 code
units as specified by the sender

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.TextQuote.entities
documentation:
Optional. Special entities that appear in the quote. Currently, only
bold, italic, underline, strikethrough, spoiler, and custom_emoji
entities are kept in quotes.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.TextQuote.is_manual
documentation:
Optional. True, if the quote was chosen manually by the message
sender. Otherwise, the quote was added automatically by the server.

This object contains information about the quoted part of a message
that is replied to by the given message.

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.ExternalReplyInfo

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ExternalReplyInfo.origin
documentation: Origin of the message replied to by the given message

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ExternalReplyInfo.chat
documentation:
Optional. Chat the original message belongs to. Available only if the
chat is a supergroup or a channel.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ExternalReplyInfo.message_id
documentation:
Optional. Unique message identifier inside the original chat.
Available only if the original chat is a supergroup or a channel.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ExternalReplyInfo.link_preview_options
documentation:
Optional. Options used for link preview generation for the original
message, if it is a text message

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ExternalReplyInfo.animation
documentation: Optional. Message is an animation, information about the animation

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ExternalReplyInfo.audio
documentation: Optional. Message is an audio file, information about the file

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ExternalReplyInfo.document
documentation: Optional. Message is a general file, information about the file

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ExternalReplyInfo.paid_media
documentation:
Optional. Message contains paid media; information about the paid
media

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ExternalReplyInfo.photo
documentation: Optional. Message is a photo, available sizes of the photo

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ExternalReplyInfo.sticker
documentation: Optional. Message is a sticker, information about the sticker

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ExternalReplyInfo.story
documentation: Optional. Message is a forwarded story

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ExternalReplyInfo.video
documentation: Optional. Message is a video, information about the video

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ExternalReplyInfo.video_note
documentation: Optional. Message is a video note, information about the video message

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ExternalReplyInfo.voice
documentation: Optional. Message is a voice message, information about the file

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ExternalReplyInfo.contact
documentation: Optional. Message is a shared contact, information about the contact

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ExternalReplyInfo.dice
documentation: Optional. Message is a dice with random value

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ExternalReplyInfo.game
documentation:
Optional. Message is a game, information about the game. More about
games: https://core.telegram.org/bots/api/#games

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ExternalReplyInfo.giveaway
documentation:
Optional. Message is a scheduled giveaway, information about the
giveaway

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ExternalReplyInfo.giveaway_winners
documentation: Optional. A giveaway with public winners was completed

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ExternalReplyInfo.invoice
documentation:
Optional. Message is an invoice for a payment, information about the
invoice. More about payments:
https://core.telegram.org/bots/api/#payments

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ExternalReplyInfo.location
documentation: Optional. Message is a shared location, information about the location

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ExternalReplyInfo.poll
documentation: Optional. Message is a native poll, information about the poll

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ExternalReplyInfo.venue
documentation: Optional. Message is a venue, information about the venue

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ExternalReplyInfo.has_media_spoiler
documentation: Optional. True, if the message media is covered by a spoiler animation

This object contains information about a message that is being replied
to, which may come from another chat or forum topic.

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.ReplyParameters

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ReplyParameters.message_id
documentation:
Identifier of the message that will be replied to in the current chat,
or in the chat chat_id if it is specified

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ReplyParameters.chat_id
documentation:
Optional. If the message to be replied to is from a different chat,
unique identifier for the chat or username of the channel (in the
format @channelusername). Not supported for messages sent on behalf of
a business account.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ReplyParameters.allow_sending_without_reply
documentation:
Optional. Pass True if the message should be sent even if the
specified message to be replied to is not found. Always False for
replies in another chat or forum topic. Always True for messages sent
on behalf of a business account.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ReplyParameters.quote
documentation:
Optional. Quoted part of the message to be replied to; 0-1024
characters after entities parsing. The quote must be an exact
substring of the message to be replied to, including bold, italic,
underline, strikethrough, spoiler, and custom_emoji entities. The
message will fail to send if the quote isn't found in the original
message.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ReplyParameters.quote_parse_mode
documentation:
Optional. Mode for parsing entities in the quote. See formatting
options for more details.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ReplyParameters.quote_entities
documentation:
Optional. A JSON-serialized list of special entities that appear in
the quote. It can be specified instead of quote_parse_mode.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ReplyParameters.quote_position
documentation:
Optional. Position of the quote in the original message in UTF-16 code
units
Describes reply parameters for the message that is being sent.

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.MessageOriginUser

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.MessageOriginUser.date
documentation: Date the message was sent originally in Unix time

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.MessageOriginUser.sender_user
documentation: User that sent the message originally
The message was originally sent by a known user.

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.MessageOriginHiddenUser

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.MessageOriginHiddenUser.date
documentation: Date the message was sent originally in Unix time

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.MessageOriginHiddenUser.sender_user_name
documentation: Name of the user that sent the message originally
The message was originally sent by an unknown user.

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.MessageOriginChat

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.MessageOriginChat.date
documentation: Date the message was sent originally in Unix time

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.MessageOriginChat.sender_chat
documentation: Chat that sent the message originally

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.MessageOriginChat.author_signature
documentation:
Optional. For messages originally sent by an anonymous chat
administrator, original message author signature

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.VideoChatScheduled.start_date
documentation:
Point in time (Unix timestamp) when the video chat is supposed to be
started by a chat administrator

This object represents a service message about a video chat scheduled
in the chat.

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.VideoChatStarted
documentation:
This object represents a service message about a video chat started in
the chat. Currently holds no information.

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.VideoChatEnded

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.VideoChatEnded.duration
documentation: Video chat duration in seconds

This object represents a service message about a video chat ended in
the chat.

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.VideoChatParticipantsInvited

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.VideoChatParticipantsInvited.users
documentation: New members that were invited to the video chat

This object represents a service message about new members invited to
a video chat.

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.GiveawayCreated

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.GiveawayCreated.prize_star_count
documentation:
Optional. The number of Telegram Stars to be split between giveaway
winners; for Telegram Star giveaways only

This object represents a service message about the creation of a
scheduled giveaway.

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.Giveaway

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Giveaway.chats
documentation:
The list of chats which the user must join to participate in the
giveaway

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Giveaway.winners_selection_date
documentation:
Point in time (Unix timestamp) when winners of the giveaway will be
selected

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Giveaway.winner_count
documentation:
The number of users which are supposed to be selected as winners of
the giveaway

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Giveaway.prize_description
documentation: Optional. Description of additional giveaway prize

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Giveaway.country_codes
documentation:
Optional. A list of two-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes
indicating the countries from which eligible users for the giveaway
must come. If empty, then all users can participate in the giveaway.
Users with a phone number that was bought on Fragment can always
participate in giveaways.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Giveaway.prize_star_count
documentation:
Optional. The number of Telegram Stars to be split between giveaway
winners; for Telegram Star giveaways only

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Giveaway.premium_subscription_month_count
documentation:
Optional. The number of months the Telegram Premium subscription won
from the giveaway will be active for; for Telegram Premium giveaways
only

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Giveaway.only_new_members
documentation:
Optional. True, if only users who join the chats after the giveaway
started should be eligible to win

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Giveaway.has_public_winners
documentation:
Optional. True, if the list of giveaway winners will be visible to
everyone
This object represents a message about a scheduled giveaway.

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.GiveawayWinners

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.GiveawayWinners.chat
documentation: The chat that created the giveaway

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.GiveawayWinners.giveaway_message_id
documentation: Identifier of the message with the giveaway in the chat

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.GiveawayWinners.winners_selection_date
documentation:
Point in time (Unix timestamp) when winners of the giveaway were
selected

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.GiveawayWinners.winner_count
documentation: Total number of winners in the giveaway

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.GiveawayWinners.winners
documentation: List of up to 100 winners of the giveaway

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.GiveawayWinners.additional_chat_count
documentation:
Optional. The number of other chats the user had to join in order to
be eligible for the giveaway

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.GiveawayWinners.prize_star_count
documentation:
Optional. The number of Telegram Stars that were split between
giveaway winners; for Telegram Star giveaways only

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.GiveawayWinners.premium_subscription_month_count
documentation:
Optional. The number of months the Telegram Premium subscription won
from the giveaway will be active for; for Telegram Premium giveaways
only

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.GiveawayWinners.unclaimed_prize_count
documentation: Optional. Number of undistributed prizes

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.GiveawayWinners.prize_description
documentation: Optional. Description of additional giveaway prize

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.GiveawayWinners.only_new_members
documentation:
Optional. True, if only users who had joined the chats after the
giveaway started were eligible to win

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.GiveawayWinners.was_refunded
documentation:
Optional. True, if the giveaway was canceled because the payment for
it was refunded

This object represents a message about the completion of a giveaway
with public winners.

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.GiveawayCompleted

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.GiveawayCompleted.winner_count
documentation: Number of winners in the giveaway

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.GiveawayCompleted.unclaimed_prize_count
documentation: Optional. Number of undistributed prizes

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.GiveawayCompleted.giveaway_message
documentation:
Optional. Message with the giveaway that was completed, if it wasn't
deleted

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.GiveawayCompleted.is_star_giveaway
documentation:
Optional. True, if the giveaway is a Telegram Star giveaway.
Otherwise, currently, the giveaway is a Telegram Premium giveaway.

This object represents a service message about the completion of a
giveaway without public winners.

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.LinkPreviewOptions

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.LinkPreviewOptions.is_disabled
documentation: Optional. True, if the link preview is disabled

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.LinkPreviewOptions.url
documentation:
Optional. URL to use for the link preview. If empty, then the first
URL found in the message text will be used

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.LinkPreviewOptions.prefer_small_media
documentation:
Optional. True, if the media in the link preview is supposed to be
shrunk; ignored if the URL isn't explicitly specified or media size
change isn't supported for the preview

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.LinkPreviewOptions.prefer_large_media
documentation:
Optional. True, if the media in the link preview is supposed to be
enlarged; ignored if the URL isn't explicitly specified or media size
change isn't supported for the preview

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.LinkPreviewOptions.show_above_text
documentation:
Optional. True, if the link preview must be shown above the message
text; otherwise, the link preview will be shown below the message text
Describes the options used for link preview generation.

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.UserProfilePhotos

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.UserProfilePhotos.total_count
documentation: Total number of profile pictures the target user has

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.UserProfilePhotos.photos
documentation: Requested profile pictures (in up to 4 sizes each)
This object represent a user's profile pictures.

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.File

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.File.file_id
documentation:
Identifier for this file, which can be used to download or reuse the
file

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.File.file_unique_id
documentation:
Unique identifier for this file, which is supposed to be the same over
time and for different bots. Can't be used to download or reuse the
file.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.File.file_size
documentation:
Optional. File size in bytes. It can be bigger than 2^31 and some
programming languages may have difficulty/silent defects in
interpreting it. But it has at most 52 significant bits, so a signed
64-bit integer or double-precision float type are safe for storing
this value.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.File.file_path
documentation:
Optional. File path. Use
https://api.telegram.org/file/bot<token>/<file_path> to get the file.

This object represents a file ready to be downloaded. The file can be
downloaded via the link
https://api.telegram.org/file/bot<token>/<file_path>. It is guaranteed
that the link will be valid for at least 1 hour. When the link
expires, a new one can be requested by calling getFile. The maximum
file size to download is 20 MB

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.WebAppInfo

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.WebAppInfo.url
documentation:
An HTTPS URL of a Web App to be opened with additional data as
specified in Initializing Web Apps
Describes a Web App.

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.ReplyKeyboardMarkup

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ReplyKeyboardMarkup.keyboard
documentation:
Array of button rows, each represented by an Array of KeyboardButton
objects

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ReplyKeyboardMarkup.input_field_placeholder
documentation:
Optional. The placeholder to be shown in the input field when the
keyboard is active; 1-64 characters

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ReplyKeyboardMarkup.selective
documentation:
Optional. Use this parameter if you want to show the keyboard to
specific users only. Targets: 1) users that are @mentioned in the text
of the Message object; 2) if the bot's message is a reply to a message
in the same chat and forum topic, sender of the original message.
Example: A user requests to change the bot's language, bot replies to
the request with a keyboard to select the new language. Other users in
the group don't see the keyboard.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ReplyKeyboardMarkup.is_persistent
documentation:
Optional. Requests clients to always show the keyboard when the
regular keyboard is hidden. Defaults to false, in which case the
custom keyboard can be hidden and opened with a keyboard icon.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ReplyKeyboardMarkup.resize_keyboard
documentation:
Optional. Requests clients to resize the keyboard vertically for
optimal fit (e.g., make the keyboard smaller if there are just two
rows of buttons). Defaults to false, in which case the custom keyboard
is always of the same height as the app's standard keyboard.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ReplyKeyboardMarkup.one_time_keyboard
documentation:
Optional. Requests clients to hide the keyboard as soon as it's been
used. The keyboard will still be available, but clients will
automatically display the usual letter-keyboard in the chat - the user
can press a special button in the input field to see the custom
keyboard again. Defaults to false.

This object represents a custom keyboard with reply options (see
Introduction to bots for details and examples). Not supported in
channels and for messages sent on behalf of a Telegram Business
account.

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.KeyboardButton

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.KeyboardButton.text
documentation:
Text of the button. If none of the optional fields are used, it will
be sent as a message when the button is pressed

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.KeyboardButton.request_users
documentation:
Optional. If specified, pressing the button will open a list of
suitable users. Identifiers of selected users will be sent to the bot
in a "users_shared" service message. Available in private chats only.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.KeyboardButton.request_chat
documentation:
Optional. If specified, pressing the button will open a list of
suitable chats. Tapping on a chat will send its identifier to the bot
in a "chat_shared" service message. Available in private chats only.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.KeyboardButton.request_contact
documentation:
Optional. If True, the user's phone number will be sent as a contact
when the button is pressed. Available in private chats only.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.KeyboardButton.request_location
documentation:
Optional. If True, the user's current location will be sent when the
button is pressed. Available in private chats only.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.KeyboardButton.request_poll
documentation:
Optional. If specified, the user will be asked to create a poll and
send it to the bot when the button is pressed. Available in private
chats only.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.KeyboardButton.web_app
documentation:
Optional. If specified, the described Web App will be launched when
the button is pressed. The Web App will be able to send a
"web_app_data" service message. Available in private chats only.

This object represents one button of the reply keyboard. At most one
of the optional fields must be used to specify type of the button. For
simple text buttons, String can be used instead of this object to
specify the button text.

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.KeyboardButtonRequestUsers

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.KeyboardButtonRequestUsers.request_id
documentation:
Signed 32-bit identifier of the request that will be received back in
the UsersShared object. Must be unique within the message

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.KeyboardButtonRequestUsers.user_is_bot
documentation:
Optional. Pass True to request bots, pass False to request regular
users. If not specified, no additional restrictions are applied.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.KeyboardButtonRequestUsers.user_is_premium
documentation:
Optional. Pass True to request premium users, pass False to request
non-premium users. If not specified, no additional restrictions are
applied.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.KeyboardButtonRequestUsers.request_name
documentation: Optional. Pass True to request the users' first and last names

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.KeyboardButtonRequestUsers.request_username
documentation: Optional. Pass True to request the users' usernames

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.KeyboardButtonRequestUsers.request_photo
documentation: Optional. Pass True to request the users' photos

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.KeyboardButtonRequestUsers.max_quantity
documentation:
Optional. The maximum number of users to be selected; 1-10. Defaults
to 1.

This object defines the criteria used to request suitable users.
Information about the selected users will be shared with the bot when
the corresponding button is pressed. More about requesting users:
https://core.telegram.org/bots/features#chat-and-user-selection

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.KeyboardButtonRequestChat

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.KeyboardButtonRequestChat.request_id
documentation:
Signed 32-bit identifier of the request, which will be received back
in the ChatShared object. Must be unique within the message

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.KeyboardButtonRequestChat.chat_is_channel
documentation:
Pass True to request a channel chat, pass False to request a group or
a supergroup chat.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.KeyboardButtonRequestChat.chat_is_forum
documentation:
Optional. Pass True to request a forum supergroup, pass False to
request a non-forum chat. If not specified, no additional restrictions
are applied.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.KeyboardButtonRequestChat.chat_has_username
documentation:
Optional. Pass True to request a supergroup or a channel with a
username, pass False to request a chat without a username. If not
specified, no additional restrictions are applied.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.KeyboardButtonRequestChat.chat_is_created
documentation:
Optional. Pass True to request a chat owned by the user. Otherwise, no
additional restrictions are applied.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.KeyboardButtonRequestChat.user_administrator_rights
documentation:
Optional. A JSON-serialized object listing the required administrator
rights of the user in the chat. The rights must be a superset of
bot_administrator_rights. If not specified, no additional restrictions
are applied.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.KeyboardButtonRequestChat.bot_administrator_rights
documentation:
Optional. A JSON-serialized object listing the required administrator
rights of the bot in the chat. The rights must be a subset of
user_administrator_rights. If not specified, no additional
restrictions are applied.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.KeyboardButtonRequestChat.bot_is_member
documentation:
Optional. Pass True to request a chat with the bot as a member.
Otherwise, no additional restrictions are applied.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.KeyboardButtonRequestChat.request_title
documentation: Optional. Pass True to request the chat's title

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.KeyboardButtonRequestChat.request_username
documentation: Optional. Pass True to request the chat's username

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.KeyboardButtonRequestChat.request_photo
documentation: Optional. Pass True to request the chat's photo

This object defines the criteria used to request a suitable chat.
Information about the selected chat will be shared with the bot when
the corresponding button is pressed. The bot will be granted requested
rights in the chat if appropriate. More about requesting chats:
https://core.telegram.org/bots/features#chat-and-user-selection.

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.KeyboardButtonPollType

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.KeyboardButtonPollType.type
documentation:
Optional. If quiz is passed, the user will be allowed to create only
polls in the quiz mode. If regular is passed, only regular polls will
be allowed. Otherwise, the user will be allowed to create a poll of
any type.

This object represents type of a poll, which is allowed to be created
and sent when the corresponding button is pressed.

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.ReplyKeyboardRemove

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ReplyKeyboardRemove.remove_keyboard
documentation:
Requests clients to remove the custom keyboard (user will not be able
to summon this keyboard; if you want to hide the keyboard from sight
but keep it accessible, use one_time_keyboard in ReplyKeyboardMarkup)

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ReplyKeyboardRemove.selective
documentation:
Optional. Use this parameter if you want to remove the keyboard for
specific users only. Targets: 1) users that are @mentioned in the text
of the Message object; 2) if the bot's message is a reply to a message
in the same chat and forum topic, sender of the original message.
Example: A user votes in a poll, bot returns confirmation message in
reply to the vote and removes the keyboard for that user, while still
showing the keyboard with poll options to users who haven't voted yet.

Upon receiving a message with this object, Telegram clients will
remove the current custom keyboard and display the default letter-
keyboard. By default, custom keyboards are displayed until a new
keyboard is sent by a bot. An exception is made for one-time keyboards
that are hidden immediately after the user presses a button (see
ReplyKeyboardMarkup). Not supported in channels and for messages sent
on behalf of a Telegram Business account.

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.InlineKeyboardMarkup

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InlineKeyboardMarkup.inline_keyboard
documentation:
Array of button rows, each represented by an Array of
InlineKeyboardButton objects

This object represents an inline keyboard that appears right next to
the message it belongs to.

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.InlineKeyboardButton

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InlineKeyboardButton.text
documentation: Label text on the button

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InlineKeyboardButton.url
documentation:
Optional. HTTP or tg:// URL to be opened when the button is pressed.
Links tg://user?id=<user_id> can be used to mention a user by their
identifier without using a username, if this is allowed by their
privacy settings.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InlineKeyboardButton.callback_data
documentation:
Optional. Data to be sent in a callback query to the bot when the
button is pressed, 1-64 bytes

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InlineKeyboardButton.web_app
documentation:
Optional. Description of the Web App that will be launched when the
user presses the button. The Web App will be able to send an arbitrary
message on behalf of the user using the method answerWebAppQuery.
Available only in private chats between a user and the bot. Not
supported for messages sent on behalf of a Telegram Business account.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InlineKeyboardButton.login_url
documentation:
Optional. An HTTPS URL used to automatically authorize the user. Can
be used as a replacement for the Telegram Login Widget.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InlineKeyboardButton.switch_inline_query
documentation:
Optional. If set, pressing the button will prompt the user to select
one of their chats, open that chat and insert the bot's username and
the specified inline query in the input field. May be empty, in which
case just the bot's username will be inserted. Not supported for
messages sent on behalf of a Telegram Business account.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InlineKeyboardButton.switch_inline_query_current_chat
documentation:
Optional. If set, pressing the button will insert the bot's username
and the specified inline query in the current chat's input field. May
be empty, in which case only the bot's username will be inserted. This
offers a quick way for the user to open your bot in inline mode in the
same chat - good for selecting something from multiple options. Not
supported in channels and for messages sent on behalf of a Telegram
Business account.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InlineKeyboardButton.switch_inline_query_chosen_chat
documentation:
Optional. If set, pressing the button will prompt the user to select
one of their chats of the specified type, open that chat and insert
the bot's username and the specified inline query in the input field.
Not supported for messages sent on behalf of a Telegram Business
account.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InlineKeyboardButton.copy_text
documentation:
Optional. Description of the button that copies the specified text to
the clipboard.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InlineKeyboardButton.callback_game
documentation:
Optional. Description of the game that will be launched when the user
presses the button. NOTE: This type of button must always be the first
button in the first row.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InlineKeyboardButton.pay
documentation:
Optional. Specify True, to send a Pay button. Substrings "⭐" and "XTR"
in the buttons's text will be replaced with a Telegram Star icon.
NOTE: This type of button must always be the first button in the first
row and can only be used in invoice messages.

This object represents one button of an inline keyboard. Exactly one
of the optional fields must be used to specify type of the button.

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.LoginUrl

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.LoginUrl.url
documentation:
An HTTPS URL to be opened with user authorization data added to the
query string when the button is pressed. If the user refuses to
provide authorization data, the original URL without information about
the user will be opened. The data added is the same as described in
Receiving authorization data. NOTE: You must always check the hash of
the received data to verify the authentication and the integrity of
the data as described in Checking authorization.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.LoginUrl.forward_text
documentation: Optional. New text of the button in forwarded messages.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.LoginUrl.bot_username
documentation:
Optional. Username of a bot, which will be used for user
authorization. See Setting up a bot for more details. If not
specified, the current bot's username will be assumed. The url's
domain must be the same as the domain linked with the bot. See Linking
your domain to the bot for more details.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.LoginUrl.request_write_access
documentation:
Optional. Pass True to request the permission for your bot to send
messages to the user.

This object represents a parameter of the inline keyboard button used
to automatically authorize a user. Serves as a great replacement for
the Telegram Login Widget when the user is coming from Telegram. All
the user needs to do is tap/click a button and confirm that they want
to log in: Telegram apps support these buttons as of version 5.7.
Sample bot: @discussbot

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.SwitchInlineQueryChosenChat

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.SwitchInlineQueryChosenChat.query
documentation:
Optional. The default inline query to be inserted in the input field.
If left empty, only the bot's username will be inserted

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.SwitchInlineQueryChosenChat.allow_user_chats
documentation: Optional. True, if private chats with users can be chosen

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.SwitchInlineQueryChosenChat.allow_bot_chats
documentation: Optional. True, if private chats with bots can be chosen

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.SwitchInlineQueryChosenChat.allow_group_chats
documentation: Optional. True, if group and supergroup chats can be chosen

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.SwitchInlineQueryChosenChat.allow_channel_chats
documentation: Optional. True, if channel chats can be chosen

This object represents an inline button that switches the current user
to inline mode in a chosen chat, with an optional default inline
query.

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.CopyTextButton

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.CopyTextButton.text
documentation: The text to be copied to the clipboard; 1-256 characters

This object represents an inline keyboard button that copies specified
text to the clipboard.

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.CallbackQuery

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.CallbackQuery.id
documentation: Unique identifier for this query

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.CallbackQuery.from_
documentation: Sender

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.CallbackQuery.chat_instance
documentation:
Global identifier, uniquely corresponding to the chat to which the
message with the callback button was sent. Useful for high scores in
games.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.CallbackQuery.message
documentation:
Optional. Message sent by the bot with the callback button that
originated the query

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.CallbackQuery.inline_message_id
documentation:
Optional. Identifier of the message sent via the bot in inline mode,
that originated the query.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.CallbackQuery.data
documentation:
Optional. Data associated with the callback button. Be aware that the
message originated the query can contain no callback buttons with this
data.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.CallbackQuery.game_short_name
documentation:
Optional. Short name of a Game to be returned, serves as the unique
identifier for the game

This object represents an incoming callback query from a callback
button in an inline keyboard. If the button that originated the query
was attached to a message sent by the bot, the field message will be
present. If the button was attached to a message sent via the bot (in
inline mode), the field inline_message_id will be present. Exactly one
of the fields data or game_short_name will be present.

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.ForceReply

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ForceReply.force_reply
documentation:
Shows reply interface to the user, as if they manually selected the
bot's message and tapped 'Reply'

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ForceReply.input_field_placeholder
documentation:
Optional. The placeholder to be shown in the input field when the
reply is active; 1-64 characters

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ForceReply.selective
documentation:
Optional. Use this parameter if you want to force reply from specific
users only. Targets: 1) users that are @mentioned in the text of the
Message object; 2) if the bot's message is a reply to a message in the
same chat and forum topic, sender of the original message.

Upon receiving a message with this object, Telegram clients will
display a reply interface to the user (act as if the user has selected
the bot's message and tapped 'Reply'). This can be extremely useful if
you want to create user-friendly step-by-step interfaces without
having to sacrifice privacy mode. Not supported in channels and for
messages sent on behalf of a Telegram Business account.

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatPhoto

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatPhoto.small_file_id
documentation:
File identifier of small (160x160) chat photo. This file_id can be
used only for photo download and only for as long as the photo is not
changed.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatPhoto.small_file_unique_id
documentation:
Unique file identifier of small (160x160) chat photo, which is
supposed to be the same over time and for different bots. Can't be
used to download or reuse the file.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatPhoto.big_file_id
documentation:
File identifier of big (640x640) chat photo. This file_id can be used
only for photo download and only for as long as the photo is not
changed.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.ChatPhoto.big_file_unique_id
documentation:
Unique file identifier of big (640x640) chat photo, which is supposed
to be the same over time and for different bots. Can't be used to
download or reuse the file.

connection. This number may have more than 32 significant bits and
some programming languages may have difficulty/silent defects in

specified identifier. This number may have more than 32 significant
bits and some programming languages may have difficulty/silent defects

signed 64-bit integer or double-precision float type are safe for

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Sticker.file_id
documentation:
Identifier for this file, which can be used to download or reuse the
file

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Sticker.file_unique_id
documentation:
Unique identifier for this file, which is supposed to be the same over
time and for different bots. Can't be used to download or reuse the
file.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Sticker.type
documentation:
Type of the sticker, currently one of "regular", "mask",
"custom_emoji". The type of the sticker is independent from its
format, which is determined by the fields is_animated and is_video.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Sticker.width
documentation: Sticker width

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Sticker.height
documentation: Sticker height

documentation: True, if the sticker is a video sticker

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Sticker.thumbnail

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Sticker.emoji
documentation: Optional. Emoji associated with the sticker

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Sticker.set_name

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Sticker.premium_animation

sticker

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Sticker.mask_position

emoji

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Sticker.file_size

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.Sticker.needs_repainting
documentation:
Optional. True, if the sticker must be repainted to a text color in
messages, the color of the Telegram Premium badge in emoji status,
white color on chat photos, or another appropriate color in other
places
This object represents a sticker.

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.MaskPosition

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.MaskPosition.point
documentation:
The part of the face relative to which the mask should be placed. One
of "forehead", "eyes", "mouth", or "chin".

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.MaskPosition.x_shift
documentation:
Shift by X-axis measured in widths of the mask scaled to the face
size, from left to right. For example, choosing -1.0 will place mask
just to the left of the default mask position.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.MaskPosition.y_shift
documentation:
Shift by Y-axis measured in heights of the mask scaled to the face
size, from top to bottom. For example, 1.0 will place the mask just
below the default mask position.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.MaskPosition.scale
documentation: Mask scaling coefficient. For example, 2.0 means double size.

This object describes the position on faces where a mask should be
placed by default.

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.InputSticker

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InputSticker.sticker
documentation:
The added sticker. Pass a file_id as a String to send a file that
already exists on the Telegram servers, pass an HTTP URL as a String
for Telegram to get a file from the Internet, upload a new one using
multipart/form-data, or pass "attach://file_attach_name>" to upload a
new one using multipart/form-data under file_attach_name> name.
Animated and video stickers can't be uploaded via HTTP URL. More
information on Sending Files:
https://core.telegram.org/bots/api/#sending-files

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InputSticker.format
documentation:
Format of the added sticker, must be one of "static" for a .WEBP or
.PNG image, "animated" for a .TGS animation, "video" for a .WEBM video

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InputSticker.emoji_list
documentation: List of 1-20 emoji associated with the sticker

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InputSticker.mask_position
documentation:
Optional. Position where the mask should be placed on faces. For
"mask" stickers only.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InputSticker.keywords
documentation:
Optional. List of 0-20 search keywords for the sticker with total
length of up to 64 characters. For "regular" and "custom_emoji"
stickers only.
This object describes a sticker to be added to a sticker set.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InlineQuery.offset
documentation: Offset of the results to be returned, can be controlled by the bot

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InlineQuery.chat_type
documentation:
Optional. Type of the chat from which the inline query was sent. Can
be either "sender" for a private chat with the inline query sender,
"private", "group", "supergroup", or "channel". The chat type should
be always known for requests sent from official clients and most
third-party clients, unless the request was sent from a secret chat

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InlineQuery.location
documentation: Optional. Sender location, only for bots that request user location

This object represents an incoming inline query. When the user sends
an empty query, your bot could return some default or trending
results.

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.InlineQueryResultsButton

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InlineQueryResultsButton.text
documentation: Label text on the button

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InlineQueryResultsButton.web_app
documentation:
Optional. Description of the Web App that will be launched when the
user presses the button. The Web App will be able to switch back to
the inline mode using the method switchInlineQuery inside the Web App.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InlineQueryResultsButton.start_parameter
documentation:
Optional. Deep-linking parameter for the /start message sent to the
bot when a user presses the button. 1-64 characters, only A-Z, a-z,
0-9, _ and - are allowed. Example: An inline bot that sends YouTube
videos can ask the user to connect the bot to their YouTube account to
adapt search results accordingly. To do this, it displays a 'Connect
your YouTube account' button above the results, or even before showing
any. The user presses the button, switches to a private chat with the
bot and, in doing so, passes a start parameter that instructs the bot
to return an OAuth link. Once done, the bot can offer a switch_inline
button so that the user can easily return to the chat where they
wanted to use the bot's inline capabilities.

This object represents a button to be shown above inline query
results. You must use exactly one of the optional fields.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InputInvoiceMessageContent.title
documentation: Product name, 1-32 characters

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InputInvoiceMessageContent.description
documentation: Product description, 1-255 characters

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InputInvoiceMessageContent.payload
documentation:
Bot-defined invoice payload, 1-128 bytes. This will not be displayed
to the user, use it for your internal processes.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InputInvoiceMessageContent.currency
documentation:
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code, see more on currencies. Pass
"XTR" for payments in Telegram Stars.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InputInvoiceMessageContent.prices
documentation:
Price breakdown, a JSON-serialized list of components (e.g. product
price, tax, discount, delivery cost, delivery tax, bonus, etc.). Must
contain exactly one item for payments in Telegram Stars.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InputInvoiceMessageContent.provider_token
documentation:
Optional. Payment provider token, obtained via @BotFather. Pass an
empty string for payments in Telegram Stars.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InputInvoiceMessageContent.suggested_tip_amounts
documentation:
Optional. A JSON-serialized array of suggested amounts of tip in the
smallest units of the currency (integer, not float/double). At most 4
suggested tip amounts can be specified. The suggested tip amounts must
be positive, passed in a strictly increased order and must not exceed
max_tip_amount.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InputInvoiceMessageContent.provider_data
documentation:
Optional. A JSON-serialized object for data about the invoice, which
will be shared with the payment provider. A detailed description of
the required fields should be provided by the payment provider.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InputInvoiceMessageContent.photo_url
documentation:
Optional. URL of the product photo for the invoice. Can be a photo of
the goods or a marketing image for a service.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InputInvoiceMessageContent.photo_size
documentation: Optional. Photo size in bytes

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InputInvoiceMessageContent.photo_width

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InputInvoiceMessageContent.photo_height
documentation: Optional. Photo height

Optional. Pass True if you require the user's full name to complete
the order. Ignored for payments in Telegram Stars.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InputInvoiceMessageContent.need_phone_number
documentation:
Optional. Pass True if you require the user's phone number to complete
the order. Ignored for payments in Telegram Stars.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InputInvoiceMessageContent.need_email
documentation:
Optional. Pass True if you require the user's email address to
complete the order. Ignored for payments in Telegram Stars.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InputInvoiceMessageContent.need_shipping_address
documentation:
Optional. Pass True if you require the user's shipping address to
complete the order. Ignored for payments in Telegram Stars.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InputInvoiceMessageContent.send_phone_number_to_provider

Optional. Pass True if the user's phone number should be sent to the
provider. Ignored for payments in Telegram Stars.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InputInvoiceMessageContent.send_email_to_provider
documentation:
Optional. Pass True if the user's email address should be sent to the
provider. Ignored for payments in Telegram Stars.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InputInvoiceMessageContent.is_flexible
documentation:
Optional. Pass True if the final price depends on the shipping method.
Ignored for payments in Telegram Stars.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.InputInvoiceMessageContent.max_tip_amount
documentation:
Optional. The maximum accepted amount for tips in the smallest units
of the currency (integer, not float/double). For example, for a
maximum tip of US$ 1.45 pass max_tip_amount = 145. See the exp
parameter in currencies.json, it shows the number of digits past the
decimal point for each currency (2 for the majority of currencies).
Defaults to 0. Not supported for payments in Telegram Stars.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.PassportData.credentials

Describes Telegram Passport data shared with the bot by the user.

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.PassportFile

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.PassportFile.file_id
documentation:
Identifier for this file, which can be used to download or reuse the
file

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.PassportFile.file_unique_id
documentation:
Unique identifier for this file, which is supposed to be the same over
time and for different bots. Can't be used to download or reuse the
file.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.PassportFile.file_size
documentation: File size in bytes

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.PassportFile.file_date
documentation: Unix time when the file was uploaded

This object represents a file uploaded to Telegram Passport. Currently
all Telegram Passport files are in JPEG format when decrypted and
don't exceed 10MB.

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.EncryptedPassportElement

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.EncryptedPassportElement.type
documentation:
Element type. One of "personal_details", "passport", "driver_license",
"identity_card", "internal_passport", "address", "utility_bill",
"bank_statement", "rental_agreement", "passport_registration",
"temporary_registration", "phone_number", "email".

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.EncryptedPassportElement.hash
documentation:
Base64-encoded element hash for using in
PassportElementErrorUnspecified

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.EncryptedPassportElement.data
documentation:
Optional. Base64-encoded encrypted Telegram Passport element data
provided by the user; available only for "personal_details",
"passport", "driver_license", "identity_card", "internal_passport" and
"address" types. Can be decrypted and verified using the accompanying
EncryptedCredentials.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.EncryptedPassportElement.phone_number
documentation:
Optional. User's verified phone number; available only for
"phone_number" type

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.EncryptedPassportElement.email
documentation:
Optional. User's verified email address; available only for "email"
type

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.EncryptedPassportElement.files
documentation:
Optional. Array of encrypted files with documents provided by the
user; available only for "utility_bill", "bank_statement",
"rental_agreement", "passport_registration" and
"temporary_registration" types. Files can be decrypted and verified
using the accompanying EncryptedCredentials.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.EncryptedPassportElement.front_side
documentation:
Optional. Encrypted file with the front side of the document, provided
by the user; available only for "passport", "driver_license",
"identity_card" and "internal_passport". The file can be decrypted and
verified using the accompanying EncryptedCredentials.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.EncryptedPassportElement.reverse_side
documentation:
Optional. Encrypted file with the reverse side of the document,
provided by the user; available only for "driver_license" and
"identity_card". The file can be decrypted and verified using the
accompanying EncryptedCredentials.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.EncryptedPassportElement.selfie
documentation:
Optional. Encrypted file with the selfie of the user holding a
document, provided by the user; available if requested for "passport",
"driver_license", "identity_card" and "internal_passport". The file
can be decrypted and verified using the accompanying
EncryptedCredentials.

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.EncryptedPassportElement.translation
documentation:
Optional. Array of encrypted files with translated versions of
documents provided by the user; available if requested for "passport",
"driver_license", "identity_card", "internal_passport",
"utility_bill", "bank_statement", "rental_agreement",
"passport_registration" and "temporary_registration" types. Files can
be decrypted and verified using the accompanying EncryptedCredentials.

Describes documents or other Telegram Passport elements shared with
the bot by the user.

classes.name: wonda.types.objects.EncryptedCredentials

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.EncryptedCredentials.data
documentation:
Base64-encoded encrypted JSON-serialized data with unique user's
payload, data hashes and secrets required for EncryptedPassportElement
decryption and authentication

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.EncryptedCredentials.hash
documentation: Base64-encoded data hash for data authentication

class_variables.name: wonda.types.objects.EncryptedCredentials.secret
documentation:
Base64-encoded secret, encrypted with the bot's public RSA key,
required for data decryption

Describes data required for decrypting and authenticating
EncryptedPassportElement. See the Telegram Passport Documentation for
a complete description of the data decryption and authentication
processes.

chats. Upload via BotFather

This object represents a game. Use BotFather to create and edit games,
their short names will act as unique identifiers.
